headings.text: Tax Analytics Solutions You Can Trust
Tax Operates Downstream — With Fixed Deadlines
The Data & Deadline Crunch in Tax
What We Do for Tax Teams
Deep Tax Experience You Can Count On
Services for Tax Leaders & Tax Operations

[Transforming Banking with UiPath: Enhancing Risk Management and Accounting Accuracy](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/transforming-banking-with-uipath-enhancing-risk-management-and-accounting-accuracy/)
[“AI” to ROI in 45 Days – \ \ Less Hype. More Help.](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/client-roundtable-how-are-tax-accounting-and-audit-teams-automating-manual-processes-and-reporting/)

[Drive your Automation Journey with \ \ Alteryx and Capitalize’s Office of Finance Blueprint](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/drive-your-automation-journey-with-alteryx-and-capitalizes-office-of-finance-blueprint/)

body_copy: Capitalize helps tax teams automate data collection, validation, and reporting so you can hit every deadline, reduce risk, and stop living in Excel-driven fire drills.
Tax functions support a wide range of obligations across jurisdictions, tax types, and regulatory regimes — each with its own rules, data requirements, and filing timelines.
Data produced by accounting, sales, payroll, and operational systems
Regulatory requirements defined externally
Statutory deadlines that do not adjust for upstream delays

When accounting close runs long or transaction data changes late in the process, tax timelines are impacted. Teams must still extract, validate, reconcile, and analyze data before filings can be completed.
The result is sustained time pressure, heightened compliance risk, and limited capacity for review — even when teams are operating effectively.
Tax teams operate downstream of nearly every core business process, yet remain bound by fixed statutory deadlines.

Reliant on upstream data readiness: Tax calculations depend on timely and accurate data from accounting, billing, sales, payroll, and operational systems. When upstream close or transaction processes slip, tax timelines compress — even though filing deadlines remain fixed.

Responsible for validation and reconciliation: While tax does not create source data, it is often the function that detects issues such as misclassified revenue, incorrect jurisdictional data, or incomplete transaction details. Identifying, reconciling, and coordinating corrections across teams becomes a recurring and time-sensitive effort.

Limited margin for error: Delays or inaccuracies in filings can result in penalties, interest, amended returns, and increased audit exposure. As a result, tax teams must balance speed with accuracy under constant time pressure.
Capitalize helps tax teams build the capability to work with their data more efficiently, confidently, and consistently.
Using tools such as Alteryx, Power BI, and cloud data platforms, we partner with tax professionals to:
Reduce manual effort and spreadsheet risk, freeing teams to focus on review, analysis, and judgment rather than data wrangling
Shorten tax cycles by creating repeatable, well-controlled data preparation and calculation processes
Improve accuracy and audit readiness through transparent logic, documented workflows, and reproducible results
Give tax leaders clearer visibility across entities and jurisdictions to support planning, prioritization, and risk management
We design solutions that respect the realities of tax deadlines, data dependencies, and audit scrutiny.
Bring together data from ERP, billing, CRM, HR/payroll, and other systems into a tax-ready data layer.
Replace manual extracts and cleanup. Build repeatable workflows to cleanse, standardize, and map data to tax requirements.
Align definitions and sources. Ensure Tax is working from consistent, trusted numbers across entities and jurisdictions.
Validate taxability and rates: Use analytics to flag mis-coded orders, wrong jurisdictions, or missing exemption data.
Focus on the outliers: Let the system process the standard cases, so your team can concentrate on what’s unusual or high-risk.
Shorten return prep: Reduce the time spent assembling and reconciling data for filings, refunds, and true-ups.
Improve provision accuracy: Automate data collection and calculations feeding your tax provision processes.
Model tax impacts: Quickly evaluate how structural changes, acquisitions, or location shifts affect tax obligations.
See the full picture: Build dashboards and reports that summarize tax positions across entities, regions, and tax types.
Automate recurring reports: Build repeatable, refreshable reporting packages for leaders and regulators.
Capture the process as you go: Document assumptions, calculations, and data sources automatically as workflows run.
Be ready for questions: Generate clear, consistent support that tax authorities and auditors can review and understand.
We understand both the complexity of tax and the power of modern data platforms.
We know where tax processes break, where errors hide, and how better data can change the game.
We start with your process and definition of success, then recommend tools and architectures — not the other way around.
We’ve seen what works across tax departments and bring that experience to your unique environment.
If your tax department is bogged down by cumbersome, error-prone processes and you’re thinking, “There has to be a better way,” there probably is. Let’s talk.
Hear insights on using agents to transform repetitive tasks like invoice processing, financial reporting, and compliance verification into efficient, real-time operations.
A practical blueprint for turning “AI” from a buzzword into a business plan that actually works. Take advantage of the special offer at the end of the webinar to get started.
Streamline your finance operations with Alteryx and Capitalize’s Office of Finance Blueprint! Automate tasks, boost efficiency, and make smarter decisions.

The New Reality for IT: More Data, More Requests, More Risk
The Questions Every IT Leader Is Wrestling With
Empowering the Business Without Losing Control
Deep IT Experience You Can Count On
Not sure where to start? Here are two fast, low-risk ways.
IT Analytics Solutions to Fit Your Needs

body_copy: Capitalize helps IT leaders design and implement modern analytics solutions — so you can manage growing data demands, enable the business, and keep your environment secure and governed.
Today’s technology teams collect and manage more data than ever before. At the same time:
Business units want direct access to data.
Suppliers and customers expect near real-time information.
Leadership wants faster insights and better decisions.

With the growing volume of data and requests, IT faces new challenges and massive opportunities. Handled well, this is the moment where IT stops being “the department of no” and becomes a true partner to the business.
Across strategy, architecture, and day-to-day operations, IT leaders are being asked to make big, interconnected decisions that shape the future.
Data & architecture choices: How do we balance centralization vs. decentralization, on-prem vs. cloud, and hybrid approaches?
Governance & control: How do we manage access, security, change control, and version control without slowing the business down?
Tools & support model: Which analytics and data tools do we standardize on, and how do we support them without creating tool sprawl?
Business users are more tech-savvy than ever, and tools like Tableau, Power BI, and Alteryx give them analytic power that used to live only in IT.
Enable self-service safely: Give users governed access to the data and tools they need, within clear standards and guardrails.
Accelerate insight delivery: Support faster, trusted analytics across functions by providing platforms, best practices, and reusable assets.
Avoid chaos and shadow IT: Reduce conflicting data, duplicate efforts, and security gaps by aligning business enablement with IT oversight.
We focus on helping IT become a strategic partner to the business through practical, scalable solutions.
We work with IT and business stakeholders to define data needs, priorities, and success metrics before talking tools.
We develop phased roadmaps that balance quick wins with long-term architecture and governance.
We help you evaluate and rationalize platforms (BI, ETL, cloud, governance, etc.) based on your unique environment.
We design data lakes/warehouses and integration patterns that support performance, security, and scalability.
We help you determine what belongs on-prem vs. in the cloud, and how to move with minimal disruption.
We embed data governance, access control, and version/change control into your architecture from day one.
We help you define which tools to support and how they should be used across the organization.
We design self-service frameworks that give users freedom within a secure, governed environment.
We help you decide what to centralize vs. decentralize and put repeatable processes in place.
We start with specific business problems — forecasting, risk scoring, optimization — before choosing ML or predictive tools.
We leverage tools like Alteryx and modern ETL to automate repetitive data prep and integration tasks.
We deliver solutions IT can support and extend, with clear documentation and knowledge transfer.
Explore where managed services can relieve the most pressure and create the most impact for your team.
A focused ideation conversation where we map your current reality — firefights, bottlenecks, and wish-list improvements.
Clarify What to Hand Off: Identify which platform tasks drain the most time and should shift to a managed partner.
Spot High-Impact Opportunities: Surface the areas where proactive monitoring, optimization, or transformation would deliver the biggest win.
Shape a Potential Model: Walk away with a clearer sense of what a right-sized managed services engagement could include for you.
Understand how ready you are for managed services — and where you stand to gain the most.
Reveal Hidden Risks: See where your current support approach leaves you vulnerable to outages, performance issues, or key-person dependencies.
Quantify the Strain: Understand how much effort your team is spending on “keeping the lights on” versus strategic work.
Highlight Value Levers: Pinpoint the areas (KTLO, continuous improvement, or transformation) where managed services could create the fastest impact.
Capitalize helps IT leaders cut through the noise, define success first, and then design data and analytics solutions that fit their environment.
Clarify business needs, pain points, and success metrics before choosing tools or architectures.
Match platforms and patterns (cloud, on-prem, hybrid) to your organization’s goals and constraints.
Create shared understanding between IT and stakeholders so solutions are adopted, trusted, and sustainable.
If you’re navigating data modernization, self-service analytics, or a flood of new technology options, you don’t have to do it alone. Let’s talk about how we can help.
Simplify Alteryx workflow documentation with AiDIN and WAM. Save time, boost accuracy, and streamline manual tasks into seamless, automated processes.

headings.text: AI Is Everywhere.   Knowing Where to Start Is the Hard Part.
Two Ways To Start Smarter With AI
Turn Uncertainty Into Insight. Insight Into Advantage.
The Real Barrier Isn’t Data. It’s Direction
The Real Problem Isn’t AI.  It’s Uncertainty.
Our Proven Approach to Responsible AI Adoption
Let’s Build Your AI Roadmap Safely
[The Future of Intelligent Automation with Agentic AI and UiPath](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/the-future-of-intelligent-automation-with-agentic-ai-and-uipath/)
[“AI” to ROI in 45 Days – Less Hype. \ \  More Help.](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/ai-to-roi-in-45-days-less-hype-more-help/)

body_copy: AI and advanced analytics promise incredible results — but for many teams, the biggest challenge isn’t the technology itself. It’s figuring out where to begin, which tools to trust, and how to move forward safely without risking data, compliance, or credibility.
Whether you’re exploring what’s possible or ready to prove value fast, we’ll help you take the first step toward AI with clarity and confidence.
A 90-minute complimentary workshop with our analytics and automation architects to identify high-impact, low-risk AI use cases. You’ll walk away with a prioritized roadmap and proof-of-value plan.
A structured, sprint-based engagement that takes one approved use case from concept to working AI model in 45 days — safely, governed, and measurable.
Capitalize’s AI & Advanced Analytics consulting services are designed to help organizations unlock the full potential of their data — transforming information into intelligence and foresight.
Assess your data, processes, and teams to identify where AI can deliver measurable impact — and define a clear path forward.
Design scalable data environments and architectures that enable analytics, automation, and machine learning across the enterprise.
Establish frameworks, policies, and best practices to ensure AI is adopted responsibly, ethically, and effectively.
Understand what drives performance and uncover root causes through data exploration and visualization.
Anticipate trends, customer behavior, and financial outcomes using advanced statistical and analytical techniques.
Run “what-if” simulations to evaluate potential outcomes and identify the most effective strategies for success.
Develop and implement custom machine learning models that address key business challenges.
Deploy, monitor, and retrain models at scale to maintain reliability, performance, and compliance.
Integrate predictive insights into workflows to drive faster, more efficient decisions.
Create intelligent assistants that combine conversational AI with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to deliver accurate, context-aware answers.
Extract, summarize, and organize information from unstructured text and documents.
Create autonomous agents that plan, act, and learn to continuously improve performance.
AI doesn’t have to be risky or overwhelming. You just need the right starting point.
We’ll identify where AI can drive value safely, quickly, and sustainably.
Your organization already has the data, but:
You’re not sure which problems AI can actually solve.
Every vendor seems to push a different “must-have” platform.
You worry about data privacy, governance, and security.
Internal teams don’t the have time or expertise to experiment.
These are valid concerns — and exactly what we help solve.
We believe AI and analytics should simplify, not intimidate. Here’s what transformation looks like when it’s done right:
Start small with achievable wins that prove value fast.
Choose technology that integrates with your existing systems, not against them.
Build data pipelines and AI models that are transparent, secure, and explainable.
Empower your people — not replace them — with smarter tools.
That’s how you move from curiosity to confidence.
Capitalize has guided hundreds of organizations from first steps to full-scale analytics maturity:
Healthcare: Integrated AI forecasting while maintaining HIPAA compliance.
Finance: Automated reconciliation with clear audit trails and governance.
Manufacturing: Deployed predictive models with zero downtime risk.
Retail: Improved demand planning and customer insights with AI-driven analytics.
Energy & Utilities: Predict usage patterns and payment risk to boost efficiency and cash flow.
A practical, proven framework that helps organizations unlock AI’s value safely while balancing innovation with governance, agility, and measurable ROI.

We begin by mapping your current processes to uncover where analytics and automation can create measurable, low-risk impact. Through a structured assessment of data quality, governance, and readiness, we identify quick wins that build momentum while aligning with your long-term vision.

Our experts design secure, governed, and ethical AI solutions using platforms like Alteryx, Databricks, and Snowflake. We rapidly prototype to validate ideas, demonstrate value early, and ensure every model meets your compliance and accountability standards.

We bring solutions to life with production-ready analytics and automation delivered through Power BI, Tableau, or your preferred ecosystem. Then, we train and empower your teams to own, optimize, and continuously improve performance as the solution scales.
→ Schedule a 30-Minute AI Readiness Consultation
Explore the future of intelligent automation as UiPath integrates agentic AI, transforming traditional RPA into adaptive, autonomous systems.

We will explore how best-in-class organizations across the utility sector use automation to optimize processes and operations, from generation and transmission to customer service and back office support.

pages.title: Comparing Alteryx and Dataiku
pages.description: Read more to compare the pros and cons of using Alteryx versus Dataiku. Make the right choice for your organization. Contact us for help., Compare Alteryx and Dataiku to find the best fit for your organization.

headings.text: Alteryx vs. Dataiku: Why is Alteryx the right choice for my organization?
Top 10 AI Pitfalls: What to Watch Out for in AI in 2026
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)

body_copy: Alteryx, Business Intelligence & Analytics
Compare Alteryx and Dataiku to find the best fit for your organization.

This article explores the similarities and differences of Alteryx and Dataiku—ultimately highlighting the unique features of Alteryx that make it the preferred analytics platform for data-informed organizations.
Here’s a quick breakdown of the differences between Alteryx and Dataiku.
Alteryx: Utilizes an integrated user interface including a tool palette, workspace canvas, tool configuration panel, results pane, and search bar.
Dataiku: Consists of three main windows: flow, datasets, and recipes.

Alteryx:  Alteryx provides different options for joining disparate data together, including a standard Join, Multi-Join, and Fuzzy Match capabilities. While configuring a Join tool, users can rename and drop fields in their data as well as review any unmatched records in the results pane.

Dataiku: Dataiku uses the join tool visual recipe. Unlike Alteryx, this tool does not contain embedded functionality to modify fields. A user would need to open the newly created dataset to make any additional modifications to a column or field.
Alteryx: Alteryx’s Browse Tool allows the user to see basic summary stats on their data like field length, data frequency, null values, and records with leading and trailing white spaces.
Dataiku: Dataiku shows the summary statistics in the datasets window. A red identifier is used to indicate values that do not match the inferred meaning of the column.

Alteryx: Alteryx allows users to modify existing columns and create new columns using the Formula tool. A single formula tool can contain multiple formulas or expressions for different columns. Formulas are processed in the order they are written so new or modified columns can be referenced downstream in the same Formula tool. Users can also save their own commonly used expressions for future use or take advantage of the pre-written commonly used expressions provided by Alteryx, such as contains(), replace(), and if/else to name a few.
Dataiku: Formulas and functions are part of the data preparation visual recipe in Dataiku. Each function is written as a new step in the data preparation visual recipe.

Alteryx: Alteryx provides users the flexibility to drag and drop tools from the tool palette or search bar into the canvas and move or rearrange as needed. Users can auto-align or evenly distribute groups of tools for added organization on the canvas.
Dataiku: Dataiku’s visual recipes are placed by the software in the flow window. There is limited flexibility on the arrangement of recipes.

Alteryx: Alteryx offers a variety of ways to learn and become an analytics expert. They provide free materials and resources like Interactive Lessons, Starter Kits, and a robust community forum to ask questions and learn from other Alteryx users. Alteryx also offers free certifications for folks at all different stages in their analytics journey: Foundational concepts, Core tools, Advanced tools, Expert, and Predictive Master.
Dataiku: Dataiku offers similar learning paths such as Core Learning, ML Practitioner, Advanced Designer, and Developer Path.

Alteryx is a software solution focused on analytics and automation. In fact, many Alteryx users transition from Excel to Alteryx because they can automate a process to parse, cleanse, transform, and enrich their data which is scalable and repeatable, unlike in Excel.

With Alteryx, users can read and connect data from many disparate sources, including flat files like Excel and CSV, databases such as SQL Server and PostgreSQL, third-party integrations like Salesforce and Google Analytics, and cloud storage such as Snowflake or Azure. Once data is read into Alteryx, it can be combined using joins, unions, appends, or a combination of these tools.

When the data is ready for cleansing and manipulation, Alteryx offers a variety of tools to make transformations and aggregations easy such as formulas, filters, pivot, and summarize. Alteryx also provides input and output anchors at each tool so you can see the changes in your data as they happen at each stage of a workflow. Having this functionality makes testing and analyzing different transformations easy and informative.

When users are ready to share their enhanced data or analysis outside of Alteryx, they can utilize the same functionality for reading data with additional connectors like Marketo, PowerBI, and Tableau. Alteryx provides a comprehensive and compatible analytics solution to integrate seamlessly with common BI tools like Tableau and PowerBI. Conversely, Dataiku software seeks to replace these integrations with its software.

By automating these once manual processes with Alteryx, analysts can focus on strategic priorities instead of basic data cleansing and preprocessing. In turn, companies can enhance their advanced analytics practice in ways that were not possible before due to constraints on resources.

Alteryx software operates in a no-code framework so that non-technical users can create ETL pipelines and analytic apps without writing a line of code. In a world where businesses are becoming more data-integrated and insights-driven, having software like Alteryx is crucial to knock down barriers between technical and business users. Tasks that once required the expertise of a software engineer can now be automated in a workflow built by an analyst.
Below is a detailed comparison of Alteryx and Dataiku:

Dataiku’s interface consists of three main parts: flow, datasets, and recipes. Flow displays a user’s current workflow. Datasets shows a list of all data sources, used and unused, imported with the workflow. Recipes store any transformations to the existing datasets. Dataiku gives users the option to start a project in a centralized space that can be accessed by multiple users to collaborate. With Dataiku’s visual recipe you can cleanse, normalize, enrich, and aggregate data without writing any lines of code. Conversely, Dataiku offers code recipes to execute user-defined code when needed. In the workflow, the action tab allows users to export, publish, or explore the dataset and provides the user access to recipes. The details icon shows information about a workflow such as created date, last modified date, and user permissions. The discussion icon is visible to all users with access to the project and pushes notifications for important updates. The lab icon displays all the visual analysis tools and code notebooks. Lastly, the timeline icon shows the workflow changes over time from when it was created to the last modification.

Alteryx Designer consists of four main components: tool palette, canvas, configuration window, and results window. The tool palette hosts all tools a user could utilize in their workflow to read, cleanse, join, aggregate, enrich, and output their data. The canvas is where a user can connect various tools to perform a sequence of actions, ultimately building a workflow. The configuration window allows a user to configure specific tools in a workflow as well as the settings behind an entire workflow. Lastly, the results window allows users to see their data as it enters a tool and how it looks after being modified by a tool. Being able to see how the data transforms at various stages in a workflow is vital when building a complex analytics workflow.

Alteryx and Dataiku have a similar way of reading data into a workflow. Alteryx uses the Input Data tool to bring in data sources while Dataiku uses its dataset tab to import. Alteryx allows a variety of input methods including Excel and csv files, cloud connections such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Azure, as well as other ODBC connectors for common on-prem databases like Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, and PostgreSQL to name a few.

1. After importing the data, Alteryx immediately provides information on the data quality such as leading and trailing white space, embedded values, and missing or null values. This information exists in Dataiku as well, but the user must navigate to the flow’s dataset window to view these details. If a user has multiple files and wants to investigate the quality of the data, the Dataiku DSS environment can be time-consuming. The Alteryx Designer interface is much more efficient than Dataiku in a situation like this as it provides the ability to show multiple files in the results window as well as the data quality of each when a user clicks on the tool.

2. Alteryx allows the user to drag tools from the tool palette and build logic with or without importing files in the workflow. This is useful for users who have an idea in mind and want to begin building an outline or first pass of the workflow. In Dataiku, it is not possible to develop a visual recipe without input data.

Nearly every analytical process involves combining data from different sources to produce deep and meaningful insights. The join tool in Alteryx can associate rows of one data source to rows of another by joining on record position or specific fields using a primary key. In Dataiku, joins are performed using the Join visual recipe. However, Alteryx natively offers multiple types of joins in the tool palette for a better user experience:
Both Alteryx and Dataiku provide custom field selection in joins.

1. The join tool in Alteryx allows users to rename, reorder, drop, and modify the data type of columns in their dataset. This can be achieved in Dataiku as well, but the user must navigate to the newly created dataset and perform these modifications there.

2. The Join tool in Alteryx stores any unmatched records in the outgoing left and right anchors of the Join tool respectively, depending on which data source that record came from. In Dataiku this can only be done by changing the type of the join.
3. The Join tool in Alteryx allows users to rename fields after a join in the configuration window of the join tool. In Dataiku, the user must go to the joined dataset to rename columns.

1. Users can evaluate the quality and health of their data in Alteryx and Dataiku. In Alteryx, an overview of the data quality can be seen at a high-level from the results pane when hovering over column names, and in depth using a Browse tool. When using a Browse tool, Alteryx displays the data summary, length statistics, and frequent values. In Dataiku this can be achieved by clicking on the individual columns and then clicking “analyze.”
2. Both platforms show valid, unique, and empty values.
Unlike Dataiku, Alteryx identifies leading and trailing whitespace when analyzing data quality and can differentiate between null and empty values in the data health summaries.
Both Alteryx and Dataiku show data preview after writing the formula.

The formula tool in Alteryx can be used to modify data in an existing column or create a new column. A single formula tool can contain multiple formulas for multiple different columns and contains a data preview to audit an expression before applying it to the entire column. Users can modify the size and data type of new or existing columns referenced in the Formula tool as well. Additionally, Alteryx users can see recent expressions and save their frequently used expressions for future reference. Alteryx also offers formula suggestions based on the data type of the column.

In Dataiku, the formula tool is a part of the data preparation visual recipe. The tool would take the user to the data preparation recipe window where they would have to write a function for each column in a new step. It can be difficult for Dataiku users to track when and where a formula is used in robust workflows since they cannot see all the formula applications in one place. Thus, the consolidation of multiple processes in the data preparation visual recipe takes away the per-step-level tracking from the users in Dataiku. Dataiku also forces a default new column name that must be changed in an additional step when creating new columns and modifying existing ones. These changes are not visible in the flow window in Dataiku like they are in Alteryx.

Alteryx Designer offers its users lots of flexibility to arrange tools as they please on the canvas. There are also pre-built options to align and distribute tools horizontally or vertically. The flexibility to customize flow design is useful for people that want to display their workflow logic visually. Dataiku does not offer this functionality. The visual recipes are placed by the software in the flow window after configuration and provide the end user no flexibility to change or move things around.
Learning Paths and Community: Alteryx vs Dataiku

Alteryx offers a variety of ways to learn and become an analytics expert. They provide free materials and resources like Interactive Lessons, Starter Kits, and a robust community forum to ask questions and learn from other Alteryx users. Alteryx also offers free certifications for folks at all different stages in their analytics journey: Foundational concepts, Core tools, Advanced tools, Expert, and Predictive Master.
Dataiku offers similar learning paths such as Core Learning, ML Practitioner, Advanced Designer, and Developer Path.

pages.title: Data Engineering & Integration - Capitalize Analytics
pages.description: Data engineering and integration consulting to unify data systems, improve pipelines, and enable analytics-ready data across your organization., Data engineering and integration consulting to unify data systems, improve pipelines, and enable analytics-ready data across your organization.

headings.text: Connect Every System, Streamline Every Pipeline, Trust Every Decision
Not sure where to start? Two fast, low-risk ways.
Build a Modern Data Foundation with Capitalize
Automated Data Pipelines Instead of Manual Fire Drills
Our Approach to Data Engineering & Integration
Let’s Connect Your Data — and Your Decisions.

body_copy: Capitalize designs and builds modern data engineering and integration solutions that connect your systems, automate your data pipelines, and deliver clean, trusted data to your analytics tools – at scale.
We use short, outcome-focused engagements to uncover value and de-risk your roadmap.
Align your team on how modern data engineering can unlock better decisions and new opportunities.
Connect Data to Business Goals: Translate analytics buzzwords into concrete outcomes like faster reporting, reduced manual work, and better visibility.
Clarify What “Good” Looks Like: Help your stakeholders understand what a modern, well-engineered data environment should feel like day to day.
Create Momentum for Change: Give leaders the confidence and language they need to champion data investments across the organization.
Get clarity on what’s holding your data back – and where the biggest upside is.
Uncover Hidden Constraints: Understand the real reasons reports are slow, inconsistent, or hard to produce.
Spot High-Impact Improvements: Zero in on the areas where better data engineering or cloud warehousing will drive the most business value.
Build Internal Alignment: Give IT and business stakeholders a shared, plain-language view of your current data reality.
From source systems to dashboards, we help you architect, integrate, and automate your data so teams can make better decisions, faster.
Evaluate systems, data flows, and technical debt to identify gaps and risks.
Architect and implement scalable cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Azure, AWS) aligned to analytics and AI needs.
Define a phased migration and modernization plan that balances quick wins with long-term scalability.
Design and build reliable ETL/ELT pipelines to move and transform data efficiently.
Implement scheduling, logging, and error handling to keep data flows running smoothly.
Replace manual data prep and exports with repeatable, automated workflows.
Connect ERPs, CRMs, and line-of-business apps to eliminate data silos.
Use APIs and messaging patterns to enable near real-time data exchange.
Create consistent views of customers, products, and other key entities across systems.
Apply validations, checks, and exception handling to improve trust in the data.
Document data assets so business users understand definitions, sources, and usage.
Set up observability to detect, triage, and resolve data issues quickly.
Tell us where your data is today and where you want it to be. We’ll help you design a realistic roadmap and build the engineering and integration layer to get you there.
Free your team from spreadsheet wrangling and last-minute data scrambles.
Time Back for Your Team: Eliminate repetitive manual data prep so analysts can focus on insights and storytelling.
Always-On Reporting: Ensure critical reports and dashboards refresh reliably without late-night heroics or one-off fixes.
Lower Operational Risk: Reduce errors, broken links, and dependency on “that one person” who knows how the data works.
Move from legacy bottlenecks to a scalable, cloud-first data platform.
Scalable Cloud Architecture: Support growing data volumes, users, and use cases without constantly re-architecting.
Ready for AI & Advanced Analytics: Put the right data, in the right structure, in the cloud so you can enable AI, ML, and predictive models.
Faster Time to Insight: Accelerate projects and experimentation by making high-quality data readily available to business and technical teams.
We meet you where you are, then design and deliver data engineering solutions that your business can actually use and sustain.
We start by understanding your goals, current data landscape, and pain points across IT and the business. Together, we define clear outcomes, success metrics, and the highest-impact use cases.

We architect your modern data platform, then design and build the pipelines, integrations, and cloud data warehousing needed to power analytics and AI. Our team focuses on scalability, governance, and automation from day one.

We rigorously test for data quality, performance, and usability, then enable your team with documentation and knowledge transfer. Capitalize can stay engaged to optimize, expand, and support as your data needs grow.
Schedule a 30-minute complimentary strategy call.

pages.title: Financial Planning & Analysis
pages.description: We help finance teams modernize FP&A with analytics and automation to improve forecasting, reporting, and data-driven decision-making.

headings.text: Give Your FP&A Team Time to Actually Plan
The Reality: FP&A Is Expected to Know Everything
Data from Everywhere, Standardization from Nowhere
What We Do for FP&A Teams
Deep FP&A Experience You Can Count On
Not sure where to start? Two fast, low-risk options.
Why FP&A Leaders Partner with Capitalize
[Scaling Workday Adaptive Planning: Lessons from Schellman’s FP&A Team](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/scaling-workday-adaptive-planning-lessons-from-schellmans-fpa-team/)

[Client Roundtable: How FP&A Teams are Transforming Their Planning, Budgeting, and Forecasting with Workday Adaptive Planning](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/client-roundtable-how-fpa-teams-are-transforming-their-planning-budgeting-and-forecasting-with-workday-adaptive-planning/)

[Drive your Automation Journey with Alteryx and Capitalize’s Office of Finance Blueprint](https://capitalizeconsulting.com/webinars/drive-your-automation-journey-with-alteryx-and-capitalizes-office-of-finance-blueprint/)

body_copy: Capitalize helps FP&A teams automate data collection, consolidation, and reporting so they can focus on strategy, scenario modeling, and answering the questions that matter most to the business.

Financial Planning & Analysis teams carry a unique burden.  They’re expected to holistically understand the organization’s financial position, model the impact of nearly any scenario, and respond to a constant stream of questions and changes from executives. It sounds complicated — but it’s even more complicated than it sounds.
If every team agreed on a simple way to pass data back and forth, FP&A would be thrilled. But they don’t.
Direct database access (when they’re lucky)
And sometimes… phone calls and screenshots
All of that information — structured and unstructured — must be cleansed, normalized, and consolidated before any real FP&A work can even begin.
We help FP&A teams modernize their data, analytics, and reporting to:
Spend less time wrangling data and more time advising the business.
Quickly model new scenarios and answer “what if?” questions.
Standardize reports and dashboards executives rely on.
Build skills in tools like Power BI, Alteryx, Snowflake, and modern FPM/CPM platforms.
We design solutions that respect the pace and pressure of FP&A, while giving you a more reliable, scalable foundation.
Use data-driven analysis of transactions, trends, and anomalies to target higher-risk areas and prioritize audit focus.
Validate that populations are complete and reliable before sampling, reducing rework and disputes later.
Provide leadership with dashboards that tie risks, controls, and results together in a clear, visual way.
Automate repeatable tests (like access, segregation of duties, and transactional thresholds) using repeatable workflows.
Generate standardized, audit-ready evidence packages with a few clicks instead of manual extraction every cycle.
Create consistent, data-backed documentation that external auditors and regulators can review and trust.
Monitor key controls and processes throughout the period, not just at quarter- or year-end.
Surface anomalies, outliers, and potential violations so your team can focus on investigation — not data chasing.
Provide Audit, Finance, and Operations leaders with up-to-date views of control health and emerging risks.
Connect ERP, HR, CRM, and operational systems into a central, governed environment for audit use.
Replace one-off data pulls with reusable, documented queries that anyone on the team can run.
Feed test results directly into standardized workpaper formats so documentation is faster and more consistent.
Explore where managed services can relieve the most pressure and create the most impact for your team.

body_copy: Business Intelligence & Analytics, Press Releases

DALLAS, TEXAS, UNITED STATES, January 18, 2023 / EINPresswire.com/ — Capitalize Analytics announced today a new partnership with DVW Analytics that eases the challenge Alteryx users have with accessing data with SAP.

Capitalize partners with technology vendors to bring value to clients from pre-sales and planning to integration and optimizing performance. The new partnership with DVW Analytics empowers SAP users to more easily access their data, helping business users, analysts, and data scientists find insights faster and create business breakthroughs.

“We are excited to welcome DVW as a partner to join us in providing exceptional service to our clients while empowering them to make more informed, data-driven decisions,” states Josh Milstein, Associate Partner of Capitalize Analytics.

Companies often struggle to access data in and across the SAP Ecosystem. As SAP has expanded their product offerings, the volume and complexity of connecting to the data has grown exponentially. DVW has solved that issue with their connectors; and it works with Alteryx, Tableau, Power BI, and replicates relational databases like Snowflake and SQL. If SAP is a key data source in your organization, the DVW solution is a no-brainer.

#### “Many of our customers have worked with Capitalize Analytics on their Alteryx implementations,” says Darron Walton, CEO at DVW Analytics.  “We are delighted to formally partner with them to bring the power of SAP to users of Alteryx in North America”.

Capitalize Analytics helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Our team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors like Alteryx, BlackLine, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, ABBYY, Workday Adaptive, and others. Our clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize Analytics works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Our offerings can help every functional area, including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. headings.text: 5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency
Alteryx for Finance Teams: Drilling into High-Impact Use Cases

body_copy: Learn how Using WAM can improve workflows by streamlining work management, enhancing collaboration, and improving operational efficiency.

When building workflows in Alteryx, many users are not generally concerned with runtime and efficiency until after the initial workflow is built out and working properly. For example, best practices would suggest including/using Browse tools through the workflow to ensure that everything is working properly and that there are no outliers that are not accounted for. These Browse tools are very useful when troubleshooting the workflow, but when the workflow is moved to production, they require unnecessary processing and can greatly increase the workflow’s runtime. Alteryx Server accounts for this by disabling Browse tools when running workflows, but Designer does not, so manually removing Browse tools can be important for any workflow that is generally run via Designer. However, not all users are aware of this, while others may forget to remove or disable them, causing increased runtimes across the board.

One of the key features in WAM’s workflow Audit Reports is automated suggestions to improve workflows. These recommendations are included at the beginning of each Audit Report and aim to increase the performance and flexibility of workflows. The suggestions to improve performance include things like reducing field sizes and disabling Browse tools to reduce runtimes by decreasing the amount of data being processed.

A lesser known way to improve runtimes is to use Formula tools instead of Data Cleanse tools. WAM will always recommend replacing a Data Cleanse tool in the workflow with a Formula tool. This is because the Data Cleanse tool is a macro that includes ten Multi-Field Formulas. For larger datasets, this can greatly increase runtimes, so it is recommended to use a single Formula or Multi-Field Formula tool to cleanse data so that Alteryx does not have to process the entire dataset more than necessary. For example, one could use a simple replace formula to remove all the whitespace in a field. In the workflow below, a large dataset of over one million records is being cleansed by both the Data Cleanse and Formula tools, but the Data Cleanse tool takes an entire minute to run, compared to the three seconds that the Formula tool takes. When cleansing multiple fields this way, a Multi-Field Formula tool can be used with a runtime closer to the Formula tool rather than the Data Cleansing tool.

Another way that WAM can help improve workflows is by suggesting changes that help with the workflow’s flexibility. To do this, WAM will always make a suggestion when a workflow includes an absolute path or an Auto Field tool. If absolute paths are used, the workflow will be unlikely to work for a different user without remapping the input and output files. This is because the file path will be different for different users, even for shared locations such as SharePoint. Instead, relative paths should be used to ensure that the input/output files are accessed relative to the workflow’s location. A useful article on relative file paths can be found here: https://community.alteryx.com/t5/Alteryx-Designer-Knowledge-Base/How-To-Use-Relative-Paths/ta-p/1160

Another way to improve a workflow’s flexibility is to use a Select tool instead of an Auto Field tool. Since the Auto Field tool automatically assigns the smallest data type and size for each tool, the data types and sizes may change when the workflow is run with different data. This can lead to issues further down the data stream. For example, a store has product codes that consist of letters and/or numbers. The store matches each day’s transactions to their database of product codes by using a Join tool. In the database, the product codes are saved as strings since they contain both letters and numbers, but one day, the store only has transactions consisting of product codes with only numbers. The Auto Field tool will change the data type of these codes to an Int16 instead of a string, which will then cause issues with the Join tool that is downstream, since String fields can only be joined to other string fields. Replacing the Auto Field tool with a Select tool that forces the product codes to be a string will solve this issue.

Using the recommendations made by WAM can help users become accustomed to building more efficient, flexible workflows by pointing out areas of potential improvement. This helps users to decrease processing times as well as help them share workflows throughout their organization.

pages.description: Learn how to easily back up workflows in WAM to protect work, maintain version history, and ensure reliable analytics governance., Learn how to easily back up workflows in WAM to protect work, maintain version history, and ensure reliable analytics governance.
pages.og_title: Easy Back-Up of Workflows in WAM - Capitalize Analytics

headings.text: Easy Back-Up of Workflows in WAM
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency

body_copy: Learn how to easily back up workflows in WAM to protect work, maintain version history, and ensure reliable analytics governance.

For automatic processes and workflows that are updated often, keeping backups is important in case any unwanted changes are made or disaster occurs. WAM can be configured to automatically generate a backup of the workflow whenever an audit report is run. This allows users to keep a copy of the workflow that corresponds to each audit report. These backups are named with a timestamp just like the audit reports, making it easy for users to determine which workflow was used to create each audit report. To specify where these backups and audit reports are saved, go to File > Settings in the WAM interface.

In the Settings window that pops up, select an Output Path by clicking Browse and navigating to where the outputs should be saved, then click Apply. Next, go to the Backups tab in the settings window and ensure that automatic workflow backups are enabled.

The next time an audit report is run, three folders will be made in the file path specified: Audit Reports, Flow Graphs, and Workflow Backups. A copy of the workflow being audited will be saved in the Workflow Backups folder.

When performing mass updates on workflows, it becomes more important to keep backup copies, since it can be difficult to keep up with every change made. WAM handles the backing up of workflows slightly differently when performing mass updates. Rather than creating a new copy of the workflow, the original workflow is not altered at all. Instead, a new workflow is created that applies the changes made using WAM.

By default, these new workflows have a timestamp included in the name, just like the audit reports. This can be controlled using the Version Control to specify how the new workflow should be named. For example, an accountant uses a workflow monthly to reconcile trial balances but updating the workflow tool by tool to specify the correct month takes quite a bit of time. Using WAM, the accountant can update most of the tools in the workflow at once – changing any month references from August to July. With the version control, the resulting workflow is labelled to show which month it corresponds to, while the original workflow remains unchanged.

Now let’s say the workflow contains quarterly calculations that reference month names to determine which quarter the data falls under. In this case, the workflow uses a formula tool to determine which quarter each month falls under like so:

WAM allows users to specify tool types as well as which items to update (such as field names or expressions). If the mass update is configured to update more than necessary, the formula may be altered incorrectly, as shown below.

Once this happens, the changes cannot be reverted using the mass update tool – thus, keeping backups is crucial when performing automatic changes to a process. Rather than going through every tool or expression in the workflow, the accountant can use the original, unchanged workflow to create a new version – this time making sure to not change the expressions. Keeping backups of workflows for every audit report or mass update ensures that users can keep track of updates and revert any unnecessary or incorrect changes that cause workflows to work incorrectly.

headings.text: Optimizing Supply Chain Analytics Using Alteryx & UiPath
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
UiPath vs. Power Automate: Which Automation Tool is Right for You?

body_copy: Explore how organizations optimize supply chain analytics to improve performance, resilience, and decision-making.

Supply chain teams are dependent on external vendors/suppliers and massive internal software platforms to do their jobs. Working with so many teams and systems outside of the supply chain’s control makes manual processes and data challenges inevitable.

Dumping data from third parties and internal systems quickly overloads Microsoft Excel, and IT teams struggle to keep up with the constantly changing requirements and ad hoc requests of the supply chain team. Tasks that should take minutes take hours and what should take hours requires days or weeks. Some processes in supply chain are so complex, they are impossible with tools like Excel due to processing times and record limits.

Robotic process automation (RPA) tools like UiPath and self-service data and analytics tools like Alteryx give supply chain teams the power to automate processes and deal with massive data sets quickly and efficiently.

With increased competition, more regulations, and tight job markets, process automation and fast data analysis are more important than ever before. The number of systems and the amount of data collected is expanding faster every day, and over the last several years, supply chains have been hit with unprecedented challenges – global conflict and pandemics, severe weather events and a changing climate, and previously unseen levels of port congestion. In the midst of this unpredictability, it’s critical to use the data you have at your disposal to make the best possible business decisions.

The benefits of doing so are clear. Optimized supply chains generally have 15% lower costs, less than 50% inventory holdings, and a faster cash-to-cash cycle by nearly three times compared to under-optimized supply chains.
Some of the key challenges we see while working with supply chain teams include:
1. Difficulty accessing and combining data from multiple sources including internal systems, vendor portals, and cloud systems.
2. Inconsistent data formats and dirty data that need to be cleansed and combined.
3. Difficulty getting the data ready for analysis because so much time is spent compiling and double-checking the data.
4. Requests for Tableau or Power BI dashboards, but the data challenges make it impossible to keep them running and current.
5. So much time spent with manual tasks and data entry, there is no time to do advanced data analysis and proper forecasting.

To alleviate the issues above, we need technology that can fetch data from anywhere, deal with the data, and push the data to reports, dashboards, internal, and external systems. Any time spent repeating manual, time-consuming, and error-prone tasks is a waste of time.
At Capitalize, we’ve helped hundreds of companies build repeatable, automated processes using two key technologies.

1. UiPath: Commonly called a “robotic process automation” or “RPA” platform, UiPath gives users the ability to train a “bot” that can mimic nearly any process that requires a person to use a keyboard and mouse. Opening applications, navigating websites, running reports, reading PDFs, filling out form, downloading files, and sending emails are just a few examples.

2. Alteryx: Can be classified as a data wrangling and data analytics tool. Alteryx can pull, cleanse, and combine data from nearly anywhere. For users who find themselves spending a lot of time fighting with Excel to merge, analyze, or output data, Alteryx is a game-changer.
How Process Automation with UiPath and Data Automation with Alteryx Can Help

By leveraging UiPath on manual processes and Alteryx on internal and external data, your supply chain teams can fully automate processes and handle large and complex data sets. You will build auditable, documented processes and stop spending valuable time performing tedious and repetitive tasks in multiple portals and Excel. Instead, your time can be spent on higher-value work: focusing on exceptions, gathering deeper analytical insights throughout the procurement process, reducing costs, and improving supplier diversity.

UiPath and Alteryx have the potential to transform every touchpoint in your supply chain, including Spend and Contract Analytics, Supplier Management, and Pricing and Compliance Analytics. It’s end-to-end. Integrating the tools together quite simply revolutionizes the process of exploring, cleaning, and combining data, for greater forecasting and visualization capability than has ever been possible. Used together, these tools help supply chain teams make fast and informed data-driven decisions, while repetitive tasks are automated in the background.
What is Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and UiPath?

RPA tools like UiPath allow you to create “bots” that replicate manual human actions. Using RPA tools, you can automate keyboard strokes and mouse clicks, log in and navigate websites or applications, fill out forms, monitor events, and call other processes as needed. This allows you to eliminate hours of repetitive tasks working with websites, and emails, pulling data from a variety of sources, managing and manipulating files, and processing documents.

RPA tools give you the ability to scale up without hiring more people, adding capacity to your existing team. Hours of manual processing time can be completely eliminated. They can dramatically increase speed and response times, reducing hours of delivery time to minutes or even seconds. And on top of all that, the automation processes dramatically reduce the risk of human errors that can result from tedious and repetitive work.

For example, one of your clients sends you a daily spreadsheet of orders, which must then be entered through a web portal or application. If there is no API, the team will need to manually type in each product and the quantity, and submit the orders. A UiPath bot has the capability to capture and document the orders, log into the product website or purchasing portal, and complete the order – all without human intervention. If there’s a problem with the order, the bot will notify the team to step in and assist.

In other cases, pulling data together for reporting can be difficult. For reports that require data from many systems, teams can end up running and downloading reports only to then be stuck manually combining everything. RPA tools like UiPath can be trained to go out to dozens of websites, plug in the correct dates, projects, and locations, export the reports to Excel, and then put them on a shared drive for the analysts to begin their work.
Once you’ve used an RPA tool to gather data, what do you do with it? Many teams pull everything into Excel and beat it into submission. It can be a daunting and onerous task.

Luckily, companies like Alteryx have built platforms that allow teams to quickly pull data from dozens of spreadsheets, systems, and APIs. You can wrangle data from various systems, clients, and vendors allowing you to cleanse and blend the data quickly. You can then transform it to create reports and dashboards. If needed, Alteryx gives you the ability to perform advanced geospatial and predictive analytics or to create highly sophisticated forecasting models.

For example: Your vendors provide weekly sales and inventory data as a report in Excel or CSV, and your historical sales records are stored in your ERP. You want to bring those datasets together and create a forecast of the products you need to ship to keep inventories above safety stock. Alteryx allows you to combine and analyze the data, and generate predictive models and forecasts, giving you a greater opportunity to ship exactly what the vendors need, exactly when they need it.

When would you use UiPath and Alteryx together? Let’s say you have several datasets, all in different places – some in vendor portals, some in cloud systems, and some in your ERP. You want to use this data to determine what to order, and when.

For the vendor portals with no access directly to the database or an API, we would build a bot in UiPath that would log into each vendor portal, navigate to the correct report, run the report, export it to Excel, and move it to the shared drive.

With the files in Excel accessible on our network, we can use Alteryx to cleanse, modify, and merge the data with the data in our cloud system and our local ERP. The combined dataset can then be analyzed and output to dashboards, databases, or files depending on the business need.
If the output from Alteryx needs to be “hand keyed” into a different system, UiPath can pick up the file and type everything in, submit each page, and send updates when it’s complete.
By utilizing UiPath and Alteryx correctly, businesses can fully automate most data, analysis, and reporting processes, only intervening for the occasional exception!

UiPath and Alteryx allow us to automate time-consuming, manual, error-prone, and monotonous processes. Each play a different role and not all processes need both technologies. In today’s high-paced, data-intensive world, understanding the tools available to automate processes is not an option, it’s a necessity.

By creating automated processes, you free up highly valuable team members so they can focus on creative ideas for cutting costs, increasing revenue, and keeping clients happy. Spending hours or days running reports, fighting with data in Excel, and hand-keying data into systems should not be how people work anymore!
With UiPath and Alteryx, we analyze a process, automate it, and put it on autopilot!

If you’d like to know more, we’d love to connect. Our team is happy to create a use-case demonstration, tailored to your specific business requirements. If you’re as excited by the possibilities as we think you will be, we’ll set you up with trial licenses, allowing us to show proof of concept before moving into production. It all starts with a conversation. Feel free to touch base today at sales@capitalizeconsulting.com

headings.text: Turn Marketing & Sales Operations into a Data Engine for Growth
Marketing & Sales Ops: Measured by the Numbers, Buried by the Data
The Revenue Ops Reality: Drowning in Data, Starved for Time
What Capitalize Does for Marketing & Sales Operations Teams
Deep Experience You Can Count On
Services for Marketing & Sales Operations Leaders
Why Marketing & Sales Ops Teams Choose Capitalize

body_copy: Capitalize helps marketing and sales operations teams automate data access, cleanup, and reporting — so you can focus on optimizing campaigns, improving pipeline, and driving revenue instead of fighting spreadsheets.
Few parts of the business are as reliant on data to show success as marketing and sales operations.
Which campaigns actually drive revenue and pipeline?
How are channels impacting brand awareness and market share?
What’s really working across A/B tests, modalities, and audiences?
Sales Operations must constantly deliver:
Accurate, timely data to guide strategy and territory planning.
Reliable pipeline and forecast visibility for leadership.
Clean, trusted CRM data the sales team will actually use.
Everyone wants answers. But getting there is the hard part.

Marketing and Sales Operations are expected to prove what’s working and steer growth — but the data they need is scattered across platforms, plagued by quality issues, and held together with spreadsheets and late nights.
Disconnected platforms: Google Analytics, social, CRM, marketing automation, and other systems don’t naturally tie together, forcing constant exports and manual blends.
Manual, error-prone processes: Analysts and ops leaders spend countless hours cleaning fields, matching IDs, rebuilding the same Excel workbooks, and maintaining one-off reports.

Always-on fire drills: Data quality battles in CRM, changing territories, and endless ad hoc requests keep teams working 50+ hours a week, acting as the human integration layer instead of strategic partners.

Capitalize helps Marketing Ops and Sales Ops cut through data chaos — connecting platforms, automating workflows, and building the skills your team needs to deliver trusted, insight-ready reporting without living in Excel.
Simplified data access: Connect key platforms (web, social, CRM, marketing automation, sales systems) so your team isn’t constantly exporting, stitching, and reformatting files.
Automated reporting & analysis: Turn recurring manual tasks into repeatable workflows and dashboards, freeing time for optimization and strategic decisions.
Stronger data & teams: Standardize and clean data so everyone trusts the numbers, while upskilling your analysts on tools like Power BI and Alteryx to build and maintain solutions in-house.
We design solutions that respect the realities of tax deadlines, data dependencies, and audit scrutiny.
Connect Google Analytics, social, CRM, and marketing automation into a central, analytics-ready model.
Replace manual exports and blends with repeatable workflows that refresh on schedule.
Analyze channel performance, A/B tests, attribution, and lead conversion in one place instead of across 10 tabs.
Standardize and enrich data so accounts, opportunities, and activities are reliable and reportable.
Build dynamic models and reports to track territory performance, coverage, and changes over time.
Deliver exec-ready views of pipeline, conversion rates, and forecasts without building them manually every week.
Use RPA/APA and tools like Alteryx to automate repetitive data prep, uploads, and reconciliations.
Set up checks that flag bad data (missing fields, duplicates, mis-assignments) before it becomes a reporting fire drill.
Create shared rules for metrics, fields, and calculations so everyone speaks the same data language.
Build Power BI and other dashboards for executives, marketing, and sales that refresh automatically.
Give stakeholders curated, role-based views so they can answer their own questions safely.
Coach your Marketing and Sales Ops analysts on the tools and techniques to extend automation and analytics on their own.
Marketing and Sales Ops teams come to Capitalize when they’re ready to get out of “spreadsheet survival mode” and into scalable, data-driven operations.
We understand campaign measurement, pipeline management, and the realities of supporting demanding revenue teams.
We help you get more out of the platforms you already own and recommend only what you truly need.
From data access and process automation to dashboards and enablement, we help you change how work gets done — not just how reports look.
If you’re stuck in vendor or channel reports and Excel, constantly rebuilding the same analysis and scrambling to answer the latest ad hoc request, there’s a better way. Let’s talk!

body_copy: Learn how Alteryx Connect integrates with Snowflake to unify metadata, improve data discovery, and strengthen governance across analytics workflows.
Integrate Alteryx and Snowflake.mp4 from Capitalize on Vimeo

Every company has large amounts of data that they need to store and analyze, but there can be many challenges in working with this data.Often the data is coming from different sources, is disorganized, and cannot be used immediately for analysis. Additionally, storing data requires significant system and resource management.
The good news – Alteryx and Snowflake can alleviate some of these pains by streamlining data preparation and optimizing database performance.

Snowflake is a cloud-based data warehouse that allows for efficient scaling and maintenance of databases. As queries are executed within Snowflake, the warehouse size can be scaled to account for different processing needs. There are multiple sizes that linearly improve the speed at which a query is executed.

The ability to adjust the warehouse size on an ad hoc basis allows users to process data quickly and systematically. Once databases are configured, Snowflake handles most of the database management such as query caching and system updates.
In short, Snowflake offers the standard data warehousing functionality in addition to optimizing storage and performance.

With Alteryx,users can easily read and write data to Snowflake databases. The simplest  way to connect to Snowflake data is using the input and output tools. The only prerequisite is to set up and ODBC connection to a specific Snowflake web server. When the ODBC is configured and Snowflake credentials are provided users can read, write, create, and delete data tables. Users can also use the “In-Database” tools to connect to a database and directly manipulate the data, without having to pull and push data from Snowflake.

The Snowflake/Alteryx integration gives users more visibility and access to their data. The Alteryx platform eliminates the need for understanding SQL, so any user can process and analyze their data. Data can be pulled from many different sources within Alteryx, then it can be blended and transformed before updating a database, which provides more consistency for repetitive data uploads. Alteryx can also be used to analyze dataor send data to other sources, such as BI tools, for additional analysis, dashboarding, or data visualization.
Would you like to see/discuss how Alteryx and Snowflake work together and learn about best practices? Send us an email at marketing@capitalizeconsulting.com, and let’s get it on the calendar!

pages.title: Capitalize Analytics Expands into Canada
pages.description: Capitalize Analytics announced its expansion into the Canadian market with market-leading data analytics and automation solutions.

headings.text: Top 10 AI Pitfalls: What to Watch Out for in AI in 2026
Power BI vs Tableau: Which One is Right for Your Business?

body_copy: DALLAS, TEXAS, UNITED STATES, April 11, 2022 — Capitalize Analytics is growing its reach and client base, expanding into the Canadian market with market-leading data analytics and automation solutions.

“The decision to expand into a new country is significant, and we’ve chosen to do so now because there is a genuine need in Canada for what we offer,” says Eric Soden, Managing Partner with Capitalize Analytics. “Canadian companies have a pressing need to streamline processes, to do more with less. We’ve been able to provide input to a growing number of Canadian companies as a trusted advisor, helping them identify opportunities to automate processes, and empowering staff with technology tools to extract better answers from their data.”

Capitalize Analytics is already active in Canada, engaging with companies in provinces across the country. Their clients represent a wide variety of industries including telecommunications, insurance, and professional services. Naturally, Capitalize also brings its depth of experience and expertise in the oil and gas industry to bear in its work with companies in the robust Canadian energy sector.

“We’re really focusing on our ability to help companies grow efficiently, and tools like Alteryx, UiPath, and Tableau help us do that,” says Soden. “Processes that were sufficient several years ago can’t scale with growth or acquisitions without tripling the number of staff. When we’re able to help our client cut the time spent on these processes so significantly – sometimes from ten hours down to ten minutes, eliminating a lot of manual intervention – growth doesn’t necessarily mean hiring more people in the back office.”

Capitalize Analytics sees Canada as a growing market, in which companies are open to new ideas, and require the expertise they offer. They are excited about their expansion into this market, representing the next stage in the company’s evolution.

Capitalize Analytics helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Their team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors, like Alteryx, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, ABBYY, Workday Adaptive, and others. Their clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize Analytics works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Their offerings can help every functional area including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. Contact info@capitalizeconsulting.com for more information.

headings.text: How to Quickly Verify Alteryx Workflow Versions?
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency

body_copy: Learn how to quickly verify Alteryx workflow versions to improve accuracy, enforce version control, and streamline analytics governance.

Since Alteryx is always improving, it is important to keep track of which version of Alteryx Designer your workflows were created in. Not doing so can lead to version compatibility errors when trying to open workflows on an older version of Alteryx than the workflow was created in, since tool configurations sometimes get updated. It is also very important to keep track of which version of Designer your workflows are created in when saving them to your organization’s Gallery, as the Gallery is unable to run workflows that were created in a newer version of Alteryx.

Additionally, new tools may be added with new versions of Alteryx. For example, the Intelligence Suite of tools were added with Alteryx version 2020.2. Opening a workflow with these tools on an older version will replace the incompatible tools as shown below.

Alteryx’s backwards compatibility allows users to open old workflows in newer versions of Designer, but it can be hard to tell when a tool was updated for additional configurations. For example, a user has several workflows made in v2019 of Designer that use the Data Cleansing tool. Running the workflows in v2020 of Designer will yield the same results, but the Data Cleansing tool now has the added capability to remove null data that the user wants to incorporate into his or her workflow. Updating each workflow will take time, and it can be difficult to tell which workflow has been updated to include this new functionality. It would be useful to know which workflows were made in v2019 so that the user can know which ones still need to be updated.

WAM makes it easy to keep track of what version of Alteryx each workflow was made in by allowing users to create an Alteryx Versions Overview report. To do so, navigate to the Reporting tab of WAM, select the workflows that will be logged, and run the Alteryx Versions Overview report in the Management section.

The report will then be saved in the “Management Reports” folder of the output folder specified in the settings. The report shows each version of Alteryx present in the selection as well as the file paths of the workflows made in each.

Additionally, each audit report contains the version of the Alteryx workflow, so for workflows that are continuously updated, it is easy to tell which versions of the workflow correspond to which version of Designer.

Using the version tracking feature of Audit Reports and Version Overview reports can give users insight into when workflows were last updated as well as which versions of Designer they are compatible with, since it can be difficult to keep everybody in an organization on the same version at all times. Compatibility issues can come up when sharing workflows with coworkers or uploading workflows to a server, so it is important to know which workflows might cause these issues beforehand. This also helps users keep track of the workflows that could be updated with new functionality or improvements that come with newer versions of Alteryx.
