pages.description: Explore best practices for scaling Alteryx Server using user-managed MongoDB to support performance and growth., Explore best practices for scaling Alteryx Server using user-managed MongoDB to support performance and growth.
pages.og_title: Scaling Alteryx Server with User-Managed MongoDB - Capitalize Analytics

headings.text: Scaling Alteryx Server with User-Managed MongoDB
This article will be a reference for those looking to understand if User-Managed MongoDB is right for their Alteryx implementation.
This brings us to our questions:
Top 10 AI Pitfalls: What to Watch Out for in AI in 2026
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)

body_copy: Explore best practices for scaling Alteryx Server using user-managed MongoDB to support performance and growth.

There are many advanced configurations available within Alteryx depending on the size and scale of the implementation. One important option for scaling, fault tolerance, and disaster recovery is user-managed MongoDB. For companies with small environments, user-managed MongoDB is probably overkill, but for those using Alteryx in a large, mission-critical environment, user-managed MongoDB is a best practice. Here’s an overview from MongoDB: https://www.mongodb.com/nosql-explained/nosql-vs-sql
1. What is User-Managed MongoDB for Alteryx?
2. How is User-Managed MongoDB different than the default installation of Alteryx Server?
3. When should clients use User-Managed MongoDB with Alteryx?
4. What are the benefits of using User-Defined MongoDB with Alteryx?

MongoDB is one of the leading database providers for application developers. Unlike a traditional RDBMS (SQL Server, Oracle, DB2), MongoDB uses a NoSQL, document-based approach.  While the SQL vs. NoSQL comparison is out of the scope of this article, a quick Google search will give you access to hundreds of articles and videos comparing the two.

Alteryx selected MongoDB as the back-end database for Alteryx Server. MongoDB will store nearly every object, configuration, and usage detail of the Alteryx Server. Workflows, schedules, user rights, server configurations, run logs, and many other items are stored within MongoDB. The MongoDB database is the backbone of Alteryx Server being queried by each Alteryx Service every time a user logs in, a user clicks on something in Gallery, a workflow schedule executes, workflows fail/succeed, etc. Like all database-backed applications, there should be little to no delay between the service requests and the database responses.

When installing Alteryx Server, users select a “Database Type” for the configuration (Figure 1). The default “MongoDB” selection will use a “free” and “embedded” version of MongoDB with limited functionality. As with most applications, embedded databases are great because they are included with your application license and work great for basic environments.
1. ##### What is User-Managed MongoDB for Alteryx?
User-Managed MongoDB is an option that configures Alteryx Server to utilize a MongoDB environment that is installed, configured, managed, and maintained by the Alteryx client.

For clients with MongoDB expertise or with environments that need to scale beyond the capabilities of the “embedded” MongoDB capabilities, User-Managed MongoDB unlocks the enterprise capabilities of MongoDB.
2. ##### How is User-Managed MongoDB different than the default installation of Alteryx Server?
The default Alteryx installation includes an “embedded” MongoDB license with limited functionality. While this license is “free” or “included” with Alteryx Server, it has limited functionality.

User-Managed MongoDB includes advanced options for scaling across multiple servers, fault tolerance, and advanced disaster recovery options. Because User-Managed MongoDB requires the full version of MongoDB, clients will have access to all of the GUI tools, reporting, logging, etc. standard with the MongoDB platform.
While User-Managed MongoDB might be great for some, there are also some considerations that make it a poor choice for others.
MongoDB costs money: To enable User-Managed MongoDB, a license for MongoDB must be procured and renewed.
MongoDB requires expertise: MongoDB is an enterprise database and requires the proper skillsets to manage and maintain the environments.

Alteryx support for User-Managed MongoDB is limited: Once a client takes control of the MongoDB environment, Alteryx support can no longer be on the hook for the configuration, maintenance, or other administration items that are fully automated when using the “embedded” license.
3. ##### When should clients use User-managed MongoDB with Alteryx?

With most technology decisions, it’s best to employ tools and advanced options when the situation requires them. Alteryx Server’s “embedded” MongoDB works great for the majority of environments. Some clients find that MongoDB becomes a bottleneck on their Alteryx environment and that the database can become difficult to manage with the limited tools available with the “embedded” license. We typically consider User-Managed MongoDB when environments have:
Thousands of workflows, schedules, versions, users, etc.
75+ concurrent users within Gallery, regularly.
The requirement of high availability, especially if downtime has been a problem.
Special security or administration requirements.

Adding the complexity of User-Managed MongoDB is not recommended unless there are scaling, fault tolerance, backup/recovery, or complex security requirements causing a need for the advanced features available within the full MongoDB platform.
4. ##### What are the benefits of using User-Defined MongoDB with Alteryx?
The key pieces of functionality that make User-Managed MongoDB a requirement for some clients include:
Replica Sets: These allow MongoDB to create replicas of the database that are used for failover and as a dedicated reading database to reduce the load of reporting queries on the primary database.

Sharding: This is a way for MongoDB to distribute data across multiple servers. This horizontal scaling mechanism increases the complexity of the MongoDB environment but creates an option for Alteryx environments with large workloads and high user concurrency when vertical scaling is no longer an option.
Enhanced Security: MongoDB has many advanced security options through different layers of encryption for data at rest or data being transferred between servers.
Enhanced Administration: MongoDB has enterprise-class monitoring, reporting, and back-up options available that are not included with the “embedded” license provided by Alteryx.

As applications like Alteryx become more and more critical to the operation of a company, new tactics are required to meet user demands. Alteryx understands that most organizations prefer an application that is easy to install, support, and maintain so the default installation includes an embedded version of MongoDB. For clients who need more control and have more IT expertise available, Alteryx has provided the ability to fully manage the MongoDB database using the user-managed installation option.

At Capitalize we always guide clients toward the minimum complexity needed to support the business. When clients begin to stress the Alteryx architecture, we look at dozens of metrics and options to figure out the best solution to meet the needs of the business. When User-Managed MongoDB for Alteryx can help clients scale their environments, we are here to help install, configure, and support the new environment. As with all our projects, we are also excited to train your internal team on the ins and outs of handling a User-Managed MongoDB installation!

If you feel like you’ve outgrown your Alteryx architecture, please contact us to help diagnose your issues. We can perform a health check and create a custom plan to scale your Alteryx Server environment!

headings.text: Three Tools CFOs Can Use to Jumpstart Their AI Journey
Databricks: Building a Solid Data Foundation
Alteryx: Automating Data Prep and Analysis
UiPath: Eliminate Repetitive Tasks with RPA
Top 10 AI Pitfalls: What to Watch Out for in AI in 2026
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
UiPath vs. Power Automate: Which Automation Tool is Right for You?

body_copy: Explore how CFOs can jumpstart their AI journey with Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath. Learn how these tools streamline data management, automate processes, and drive smarter financial decision-making for measurable business impact.
Advanced AnalyticsAgentic AIAICFOGenerative AIPredictive AI

As a CFO, your role extends well beyond managing finances – you’re at the forefront of driving automation, improving efficiency, and shaping strategic transformation. With countless AI tools and platforms available, it’s challenging to identify where to begin. From streamlining data collection to leveraging machine learning, the opportunities are vast, but finding the technologies that deliver real value requires a focused approach.

Let’s demystify one of the most common buzzwords: Artificial Intelligence (AI). At its core, AI means having computers perform tasks traditionally done by humans. The real appeal? Saving time. Take ChatGPT, for example – it helps generate text quickly and efficiently, freeing up valuable hours. Similarly, implementing AI solutions allows you to tackle tasks faster and more effectively, maximizing productivity.

While Hollywood portrays AI as futuristic or even apocalyptic, the reality today is far more practical. Today, AI is about using smart tools to solve real-world problems and drive value. From foundational data collection to advanced automation and decision-making, the AI journey offers transformative potential at every step.

To stay ahead, you need to cut through the noise and focus on technologies that deliver meaningful impact. At Capitalize, we’ve seen how time-saving automation and smarter data management can revolutionize businesses. Let’s dive into three powerful technologies – Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath – that can help your finance team streamline operations, make better decisions, and lay the groundwork for AI-driven success.

The key to unlocking the full potential of AI lies in a strong data strategy – a clear plan for collecting, organizing, and using your organization’s data effectively. Two of the most common approaches to data storage are data lakes and data warehouses. Think of a data lake as a vast reservoir that stores raw, unfiltered data, while a data warehouse acts more like a refined library that organizes and processes data for easy access. Databricks offers a Lakehouse platform that combines the flexibility of data lakes with the structured efficiency of data warehouses, enabling seamless data integration and accessibility.
Consolidate disparate data sources (ERP systems, financial research data, external market data) into a centralized platform.
Interact with the AI/BI Genie to engage with your data using natural language (aka plain English), enabling you to create dashboards, uncover insights, and accelerate data-driven initiatives.

Take advantage of AutoML to develop baseline machine learning models (such as sales forecasting) with a glassbox approach where the code is generated automatically and ready to be refined to best fit your desired use case.
Use the context-aware Databricks Assistant for help with generating, describing, or debugging code.
Manage the entire lifecycle of Generative AI models, from data preparation and model training to deployment and monitoring, ensuring a seamless and scalable approach to AI implementation.
By centralizing data, leveraging AI-driven insights, and simplifying the adoption of machine learning, you can move beyond traditional analytics to drive strategic initiatives.

Your finance team often spends hours – or even days – wrangling data in Excel, leaving little time for strategic analysis. Enter Alteryx, a no-code/low-code platform that democratizes analytics and automates tedious data preparation.
Automate data preparation and blending across multiple sources, reducing manual effort and errors while speeding up critical processes (like month-end reporting and profitability reviews).
Streamline repetitive workflows with intuitive drag-and-drop tools, empowering your team to focus on higher-value tasks.

Utilize AI-driven data transformation suggestions to identify the best ways to structure, clean, and optimize your datasets, making workflows more efficient and reducing the manual effort required for data preparation.
Integrate easy-to-use predictive analytics and machine learning to identify trends, forecast outcomes, and guide strategic initiatives without requiring advanced coding skills.
Leverage Alteryx Auto Insights to uncover actionable, AI-driven insights and visualizations that enable faster, data-backed decisions.
By enhancing data accessibility, making advanced analytics more approachable, and saving time through automation, you can uncover new opportunities for growth and efficiency.

You may be thinking, “Isn’t RPA just automation? What does that have to do with AI?” While it’s true that Robotic Process Automation (RPA) focuses on automating repetitive tasks, modern platforms like UiPath have integrated powerful AI capabilities that go far beyond traditional automation.

Leverage AI-driven Intelligent Document Processing to extract, validate, and categorize financial data from unstructured documents like invoices, receipts, and contracts, reducing manual effort and improving accuracy.
Utilize AI models for process optimization, enabling bots to intelligently adapt to complex workflows and make data-driven decisions.
Combine RPA with natural language processing (NLP) to handle text-heavy tasks, such as reading and interpreting compliance reports or contracts.
Seamlessly integrate with your existing ERP systems, CRMs, and other tools to streamline workflows and eliminate manual data entry.

Imagine a bot powered by AI not only logging into your ERP but also analyzing patterns in financial data, identifying anomalies, and preparing actionable reports for your team. UiPath transforms finance operations by blending automation with AI to deliver smarter, faster, and more accurate results.

Navigating the AI landscape can feel overwhelming, but focusing on the right tools makes all the difference. Databricks, Alteryx, and UiPath each offer unique capabilities that empower finance teams to unlock the full potential of their data and streamline operations. Whether it’s Databricks’ robust data infrastructure and AutoML tools, Alteryx’s user-friendly analytic tools and low-code predictive capabilities, or UiPath’s intelligent automation and document processing, these platforms enable CFOs to move beyond traditional methods and embrace data-driven decision-making.

By integrating these solutions into their tech stack, CFOs can not only optimize day-to-day processes but also position their organizations to thrive in an increasingly data-centric world. At Capitalize Consulting, we specialize in helping finance leaders implement these tools to drive measurable results.

body_copy: Capitalize named Alteryx Global Partner of the Year, honored for driving innovation and results in AI, analytics, and automation.
Capitalize celebrated for their exceptional contributions in the 2025 Alteryx Partner Awards

Dallas, TX — May 22, 2025 — Capitalize was recently named a Partner Award winner by Alteryx, Inc ., a leading AI and data analytics company. The annual Alteryx Partner Awards recognize Alteryx partners for their significant achievements in advancing AI and analytics, empowering organizations to unlock data-driven insights and drive meaningful business outcomes.

“We are incredibly proud to partner with the recipients of the 2025 Alteryx Partner Awards,” said Steven Birdsall, Chief Revenue Officer at Alteryx. “These outstanding organizations exemplify innovation and excellence, redefining how businesses utilize analytics and AI to overcome complex challenges. Their commitment to advancing data-driven strategies underscores the critical role intelligent decision-making plays in shaping the future of business. At Alteryx, we celebrate their achievements and are inspired to continue driving innovation.”

The Alteryx Partner Awards recognize Alteryx partners who excel at addressing the complexities businesses face today. These partners have made significant contributions to Alteryx, delivering impactful customer experiences through actionable data insights. Together, we remain focused on our continued collaboration as we advance the next generation of analytics and AI solutions.

“Being named Alteryx Global Partner of the Year for the seventh year in a row is an incredible honor and a testament to the consistency, creativity, and commitment of our team,” says Eric Soden, Managing Partner. “This recognition reflects the impact we’ve made together—solving real problems for clients and delivering meaningful results. I’m proud of what we’ve built with Alteryx, and even more excited about where we’re headed next.”

Capitalize helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Our team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors, like Alteryx, Databricks, UiPath, Tableau, Power BI, Workday Adaptive Planning, and others. We are SOC 2 Type II certified and work with companies of all sizes to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Our offerings can help every functional area, including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. body_copy: Business Intelligence & Analytics, Power BI, Tableau
Compare Power BI vs Tableau to find the best BI tool for your needs. Explore key features, integrations, scalability, and costs to make an informed decision.

Choosing between Power BI and Tableau can be a daunting decision, especially when both platforms offer powerful data visualization and business intelligence (BI) capabilities. Whether you’re looking to enhance efficiency, automate reporting, reduce costs, or increase flexibility, understanding the strengths and limitations of each tool is crucial.

Power BI, a Microsoft product, is known for its ease of use and seamless integration with the Microsoft ecosystem. Tableau, on the other hand, is widely recognized for its superior data visualization capabilities and flexibility in handling large and complex datasets. But which one is the best fit for your business?
This article provides a detailed comparison of Power BI vs Tableau across key features, use cases, and real-world applications to help you make an informed decision.
Power BI vs Tableau: Which One is Right for Your Business? from Capitalize on Vimeo
Ease of Use | User-friendly interface with a short learning curve | Highly flexible visuals but with a steeper learning curve
Integration Capabilities | Seamless Microsoft ecosystem integrations (Azure, Excel, SharePoint) | Wide range of data connectors; may need extra configuration or connectors
Scalability | Scales effectively for SMBs and mid-market; part of the Microsoft stack | Large enterprises and complex data
Automation & AI | Built-in AI visuals, natural language Q&A, automation via Power BI workspace | Advanced analytics and AI through Tableau integrations; strong visual exploration
Cost & Licensing | Generally lower entry point; favors those with Microsoft stack | Costs are higher for enterprise-level deployments
Best For | Finance, Operations, SMBs, Microsoft-centric businesses | Large enterprises and Data Science teams

Power BI: Power BI offers a straightforward setup, especially for teams already using Microsoft tools. Its intuitive interface helps non-technical users build dashboards quickly, making it a strong choice for those seeking a shorter learning curve.

Tableau: Tableau is renowned for its robust visualization capabilities but comes with a steeper learning curve. While its drag-and-drop interface is powerful, teams may need more time or business intelligence consulting to unlock its full potential.

Key Takeaway: When evaluating Tableau vs Power BI pros and cons, businesses that need rapid deployment and a familiar feel often prefer Power BI, while those seeking highly customizable, in-depth visuals may choose Tableau.

Native Integrations: Power BI naturally integrates with Microsoft tools (Azure, Excel, SharePoint, Teams). This proves especially beneficial for organizations already using Microsoft products, as they can leverage single sign-on (SSO) and unified security models.
API Access: Developers can connect various data sources via Power BI’s REST APIs, enabling custom data ingestion or embedded reports into third-party applications and websites.

Compatibility with ERPs & CRMs: Power BI offers built-in connectors for widely used enterprise tools, such as Dynamics 365, Salesforce, and SAP. This makes data extraction more seamless, eliminating the need for complicated workarounds.

Enterprise Tools: If your organization relies on Windows Active Directory for user management or Azure services for hosting, you can provision users, automate data refreshes, and govern data access policies natively.

Wide Data Connector Library: Tableau shines in its flexibility, offering an extensive list of connectors (Salesforce, Google Analytics, Oracle, SAP, and more). These prebuilt connectors often streamline the process of loading data from disparate sources.

API & Extensibility: Tableau provides a robust API framework, allowing developers to build custom scripts or applications around the Tableau environment – like automating workbook publication or integrating Tableau visuals into internal portals.

Compatibility with ERPs & CRMs: Thanks to partnerships with multiple vendors, Tableau connects directly with popular ERP and CRM solutions. Organizations using a range of enterprise systems can unify data from these tools in a single analytics layer.

Tableau Implementation: While the integration options are vast, implementing Tableau for a complex multi-cloud or on-premises environment may require additional expertise. Nevertheless, once set up, Tableau’s ecosystem can handle a variety of enterprise data flows.

Key Takeaway: Choosing between Tableau and Power BI for integration depends heavily on your existing tech stack and the variety of systems you need to connect. If you are heavily invested in Microsoft solutions and want native compatibility, Power BI is the smoother fit. If you require a broader set of connectors for a complex environment of ERPs, CRMs, or database platforms, Tableau’s extensive connector library may offer more flexibility (though potentially more set-up time).

Power BI: Built to scale for small to mid-sized organizations, Power BI can grow alongside your data needs, especially within a Microsoft-centric environment. It suits businesses that expect steady growth or rely heavily on Azure-based solutions.

Tableau: Tableau is often favored by larger enterprises with complex data flows. Its architecture supports significant data volumes and multi-server deployments, making it more suitable for global teams and advanced business intelligence solutions.

Key Takeaway: Tableau vs Power BI key differences in scalability boil down to enterprise complexity. For streamlined growth, Power BI is often sufficient, but Tableau shines for organizations handling massive or intricate datasets.

Power BI: Microsoft Power BI offers a robust set of AI-driven capabilities right out of the box. The platform’s Q&A feature lets you query your data in natural language to quickly surface key insights, while built-in visualizations such as Key Influencers can automatically identify which factors drive certain metrics. For automation, Power BI’s workspace environment allows you to schedule data refreshes, configure on-premises data gateways, and manage a variety of data sources to keep your dashboards up to date. Furthermore, integration with Microsoft Power Automate supports advanced workflows, enabling triggers and actions across multiple services whenever specific data conditions are met. These capabilities collectively help organizations maintain continuously refreshed analytics, streamline processes, and uncover actionable insights with minimal manual effort.

Tableau: Tableau offers a variety of native AI and automation features that can be expanded with external integrations, making it a versatile platform for organizations with different levels of analytics needs. Built-in capabilities like “Explain Data” provide automated insights into what may be driving trends in your visualizations, while “Ask Data” allows users to query data in natural language. For more sophisticated or tailored machine learning solutions, Tableau can integrate with languages such as Python or R, enabling teams to develop and run custom models within their existing analytics workflows.
Key Takeaway: If you seek “hands-free” automation with minimal setup, Power BI might be ideal. For teams requiring extensive customization or advanced analytics, Tableau delivers deeper flexibility.

Power BI: Power BI offers both free and paid tiers: a free version for individual use and Power BI Pro for monthly per-user collaboration and dashboard sharing. Power BI Premium uses capacity-based rather than per-user licensing, allowing broader deployment for large teams. While it follows a subscription-based model, organizations must choose carefully between user-based (Pro) or capacity-based (Premium) to avoid hidden fees and unexpected Azure cloud costs for larger data processing.

Tableau: Tableau divides its licenses into three main categories – Creator, Explorer, and Viewer – each with different capabilities and costs. Although it historically supported perpetual licenses, it has largely shifted to a subscription-first model. In bigger enterprises, determining the right number of Creator, Explorer, and Viewer seats can become complex. Additional expenses can arise if you need extra servers for large data deployments, on-premises hosting infrastructure, or consulting for advanced implementations.

Key Takeaway: Power BI generally offers a more budget-friendly path for organizations already in the Microsoft ecosystem, while Tableau’s tiered licensing may be more expensive but offers greater flexibility for complex data environments. Ultimately, your choice will depend on your budget, existing tech stack, and the sophistication of analytics your business requires.
When selecting between Power BI and Tableau, consider the following:
You need an easy-to-use BI tool with a short learning curve.
Your company is heavily invested in the Microsoft ecosystem (Azure, Excel, SharePoint).
You’re looking for an affordable and scalable solution for SMBs or mid-market organizations.
You require built-in AI and automation with minimal customization.
You need advanced data visualization and analytics capabilities.
Your business deals with large-scale, complex datasets across multiple platforms.
You require extensive flexibility in data integration beyond Microsoft tools.
You have the budget for a premium BI solution with enterprise-level scalability.

Selecting the right BI tool depends on your company’s data strategy, existing tech stack, and scalability needs. If you’re still unsure, our experts can help you assess the best fit for your business intelligence and analytics goals. Contact us today for a consultation!

pages.og_title: The Future of Intelligent Automation with Agentic AI and UiPath

headings.text: The Future of Intelligent Automation with Agentic AI and UiPath
The New Era of Automation: RPA Meets Agentic AI
Industry Applications for UiPath’s Agentic Bots
What’s Next: The Future of Autonomous Digital Workforces
UiPath vs. Power Automate: Which Automation Tool is Right for You?
UiPath vs Automation Anywhere: Which RPA Tool Is Right for You?

body_copy: Explore the future of intelligent automation as UiPath integrates agentic AI, transforming traditional RPA into adaptive, autonomous systems.

The landscape of intelligent automation is rapidly evolving, driven by breakthroughs in both Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and agentic artificial intelligence (agentic AI). While traditional RPA has automated repetitive, rule-based tasks, agentic AI represents a leap forward, enabling machines to perceive, reason, and learn independently. This shift is ushering in a new era of adaptive automation, where bots can manage both routine tasks and complex, decision-intensive processes. As organizations strive for greater operational efficiency, the fusion of RPA and agentic AI promises transformative potential, allowing businesses to move from mere task automation to intelligent, context-aware decision-making.

Over the past decade, RPA has played a pivotal role in streamlining repetitive tasks like data entry and form processing. However, these early automation solutions were primarily rule-based, relying on predefined commands without the ability to adapt. The emergence of agentic bots marks a significant evolution, shifting automation from rigid task execution to flexible, autonomous decision-making.

Agentic AI integrates advanced technologies such as natural language processing (NLP), machine learning (ML), and contextual awareness, making bots more interactive and effective. Unlike their rule-based predecessors, agentic bots can comprehend context, adapt to changing circumstances, and make real-time decisions within established parameters. This enhanced capability relieves human workers of routine oversight, enabling them to focus on strategic initiatives and driving innovation across the organization.

By bridging automation with true autonomy, this new wave of technology promises more dynamic workflows, increased productivity, and the potential to redefine business operations. As agentic bots take on more complex roles, businesses can expect a greater return on investment through faster decision-making, continuous process optimization, and more meaningful human-machine collaboration.

UiPath is at the forefront of integrating agentic AI into its automation platform, marking a major shift from traditional RPA toward more autonomous “agentic automation.” This innovation enables AI agents to handle complex, end-to-end workflows with minimal human intervention, making decisions, setting goals, and dynamically adapting to evolving situations.

Key to this transition is UiPath’s introduction of tools like Agent Builder™, a low-code interface within UiPath Studio that allows users to create AI agents from scratch or through templates. These agents are designed to work seamlessly alongside existing RPA bots, human-in-the-loop workflows, and real-time decision-making processes. Another notable tool is UiPath Autopilot, a cross-platform AI agent that offers a conversational interface, empowering users to interact with agents for tasks such as document analysis, data entry, and more.

The goal is to expand automation capabilities across enterprises by enabling more sophisticated interactions between AI agents, RPA robots, and human users, improving overall productivity and decision-making. This approach positions UiPath as a leader in the journey toward true intelligent automation, offering tools that not only enhance productivity but also drive continuous innovation.
UiPath’s agentic AI introduces five transformative benefits designed to redefine what RPA can achieve:

Context-Aware Decision-Making: One of the most compelling features of agentic bots is their ability to understand and process complex situations, adjusting responses based on context. For example, instead of merely following a pre-programmed workflow, an agentic bot can interpret variables like user behavior, data anomalies, or changing compliance requirements and act accordingly.

Real-Time Adaptability: Traditional bots often struggle with unexpected variables or unstructured data. Agentic bots, however, thrive in dynamic environments, adjusting workflows as new information becomes available. Imagine a supply chain process where demand spikes unpredictably; agentic bots can adjust inventory levels, update shipping schedules, and trigger additional production – all in real time.

Enhanced Accuracy and Efficiency: By reducing the need for human intervention, agentic bots offer superior speed and precision. They can process more tasks in less time and with fewer errors. This doesn’t just improve performance metrics; it also drives cost savings by streamlining operations and reducing manual workload.

Proactive Issue Resolution: Unlike traditional bots, which need human intervention to address errors, agentic bots can identify and resolve issues autonomously. For instance, in a customer service workflow, an agentic bot can detect a service delay, troubleshoot the problem, and either resolve it or escalate it automatically, ensuring minimal downtime and better user experiences.

Continuous Self-Learning: What sets agentic bots apart is their self-learning capability. Unlike traditional RPA, which relies heavily on human updates, agentic bots can learn from their past actions, adapt workflows, and refine decision-making over time. This is possible through advanced AI models that analyze patterns, evaluate outcomes, and improve decision accuracy.
Here’s a look at some specific applications across various sectors:

Banking & Finance: The finance industry thrives on accurate, timely, and compliant operations. Agentic bots can automate complex processes such as compliance checks, fraud detection, and even intelligent financial advising. With the ability to adapt to changing regulations and analyze large volumes of transactional data, agentic bots not only ensure compliance but also provide valuable insights that drive decision-making.

Healthcare: Healthcare is an industry defined by constant change, from patient data updates to treatment protocols. Agentic bots can manage adaptive scheduling, monitor patient data, and even make proactive treatment recommendations based on real-time data analysis. This capability not only optimizes operations but also improves patient outcomes by ensuring timely interventions.

Supply Chain: In supply chain management, where efficiency and responsiveness are critical, agentic bots can optimize processes like real-time inventory management, demand forecasting, and autonomous route optimization. By analyzing patterns and adjusting processes dynamically, these bots reduce waste, enhance resource allocation, and improve the overall speed and accuracy of supply chain operations.

Customer Service: Customer service is ripe for transformation with agentic bots. From intelligent case routing to real-time sentiment analysis and proactive issue resolution, these bots elevate the customer experience by anticipating needs, resolving issues faster, and adapting to individual customer behaviors.

Manufacturing: Agentic bots can enhance manufacturing processes through predictive analytics, optimized production schedules, and automated quality checks. They don’t just react to data – they predict potential issues and adjust processes proactively, minimizing downtime and waste.

Insurance: In the insurance sector, agentic bots automate processes like claims analysis, fraud detection, and policy personalization. By learning from past data and decisions, these bots adapt to regulatory changes and emerging risks, ensuring compliance and improving customer satisfaction.
While the potential of agentic bots is enormous, successful implementation requires careful planning and strategy. Key considerations include:

Integration: Agentic bots must be seamlessly integrated with existing systems to function effectively. This often requires robust API development, data mapping, and interoperability assessments to ensure compatibility across platforms.

Change Management: The shift from traditional RPA to agentic automation involves significant changes in workflows and roles. Organizations must prepare their teams for this shift by fostering a culture of digital adoption and providing training to manage and collaborate with these autonomous agents.

Human Oversight: While agentic bots operate independently, they still require human oversight to ensure compliance, accuracy, and ethical behavior. Setting clear parameters and establishing monitoring protocols are essential to maintaining control and accountability.

The rise of agentic bots is more than just an upgrade; it’s a fundamental change in how we approach digital transformation. As they continue to evolve, these bots will become integral components of the digital workforce, driving greater autonomy, agility, and innovation. They represent not only the future of RPA but also the future of work itself, enabling companies to be more adaptable, resilient, and proactive.

The next few years will see agentic bots playing central roles in complex operations, making decisions that drive real-time business outcomes, and creating new opportunities for growth. UiPath, as a leader in this space, is pushing the boundaries of what’s possible, paving the way for a more autonomous and intelligent digital ecosystem.

As a UiPath Premium Partner, we’re excited to help organizations leverage agentic AI to achieve breakthrough results in efficiency, accuracy, and cost savings. Ready to see what agentic automation can do for your business? Connect with our team to start your transformation journey today! Contact Us for more information.

body_copy: Capitalize earns Alteryx Americas and Marketplace Partner of the Year, highlighting leadership in analytics automation and data solutions.
Capitalize Named Alteryx Americas and Marketplace Partner of the Year
Capitalize recognized for their remarkable achievements with two 2024 Maveryx Excellence Awards

Dallas, TX. June 1, 2024 – Capitalize was recently recognized for two Maveryx awards, the 2024 Americas Partner of the Year and 2024 Marketplace Partner of the Year by Alteryx, Inc., a leader in AI for enterprise analytics. The annual Maveryx Excellence Awards recognize Alteryx partners and customers for their outstanding commitment to helping organizations democratize analytics and drive business outcomes through data-driven insights.

“We are truly honored to collaborate with the recipients of the 2024 Maveryx Excellence Awards,” said Scott Van Valkenburgh, senior vice president of global alliances and channels, Alteryx. “These awardees consistently challenge the norm and promote the democratization of analytics within their organizations and the wider community. They have advanced their data strategies and continuously inspired those around them to leverage analytics and AI to address critical business challenges.”

The Americas Partner of the Year and Marketplace Partner of the Year awards honor exceptional partners who understand the challenges businesses face as they become data-driven, as well as their significant contribution to the success and growth of Alteryx over the past year, providing customers with impactful experiences through data insights.

“It’s an honor to celebrate Capitalize’s fifth year in a row as an Alteryx “Partner of the Year,” said Eric Soden, Managing Partner at Capitalize.  “Our companies work so well together because of our joint mission to bring our clients better data, analytics, and automation solutions. Companies often struggle to find solutions that “move the needle, but with the combination of the Alteryx platform with Capitalize services/training, clients save thousands of hours, reduce risks across their businesses, upskill their employees, and have better access to data to help them run their businesses. With a value proposition like that, it’s hard not to win together!”

Capitalize Analytics helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Our team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors like Alteryx, BlackLine, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, Databricks, Workday Adaptive, and others. Our clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize Analytics works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Our offerings can help every functional area, including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. For more information, please visit capitalizeanalytics.com

headings.text: Scaling Self-Service Analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud

body_copy: Scale self-service analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud to improve data governance, trust, and insight delivery across your organization.
Scaling Self-Service Analytics with Collibra’s Data Intelligence Cloud.mp4 from Capitalize on Vimeo

Self-service analytical solutions have changed the way companies access, analyze, and share data. People are able to pull data from nearly any source, manipulate it, and share it with anyone in minutes or hours instead of days or weeks.
However, with all great progress comes new challenges. Companies that provide self-service access can struggle with answering questions such as:
Who gave these folks access to these systems?
Do the users understand what the data is telling them, what’s already been filtered, and what calculations have already occurred?
Collibra has designed a platform to help people find, access, understand, and control their data, easing the pain of large self-service environments. The solution includes:
Data Catalog – Find, access, and trust the organization’s data with a simple interface that surfaces data assets across the organization.
Data Governance – Organize, document, and certify data while collaborating with other users of that same data – in a controlled way.
Data Lineage – See the source and any modifications that happen to your data as it makes its way to reports, dashboards, etc.
Data Privacy – Ensure your data is staying private and you’re adhering to ever-changing privacy laws.
Please join Capitalize Analytics and Collibra in this webinar where we will discuss these challenges and how the Collibra platform makes self-service more efficient and safer.

body_copy: Capitalize is recognized as a UiPath Agentic Automation Fast Track Partner for its leadership in AI-powered, end-to-end enterprise automation.

Dallas, TX/USA — August 7, 2025 — Capitalize today announced it has been honored with a badge of distinction for being a UiPath Agentic Automation Fast Track Partner. The badge of distinction for UiPath Agentic Automation Fast Track Partners demonstrates an outstanding commitment from UiPath partners to deliver an orchestrated enterprise for customers through a combination of AI agents, robots, people, AI models and other tools to orchestrate and automate end-to-end enterprise processes.

People have long turned to robots for automating repetitive tasks, freeing themselves to focus on more fulfilling and important work. Businesses have embraced automation to streamline operations, cut costs, and improve customer experiences. However, traditional automations have been less effective on complex, unstructured processes where intelligent decision-making is needed. Now, a new era for automation — agentic automation — accelerates these advancements by combining AI, automation, and orchestration, giving agents the power to plan, work, and make decisions with minimal human oversight.

Agentic automation expands the automation potential of all organizations by placing focus not just on individual tasks, but on entire end-to-end processes. UiPath is the industry’s first enterprise-grade platform for agentic automation designed to transform the way humans work. The UiPath Platform accelerates the shift toward a new era of agentic automation—one where agents, robots, people, and models integrate seamlessly to enable autonomous processes and smarter decision-making.

“As a Platinum UiPath Partner, we’re honored to be recognized as a UiPath Agentic Automation Fast Track Partner,” said Eric Soden, Managing Partner at Capitalize. “This badge reflects our continued investment in next-generation automation and our commitment to helping clients harness the full power of the UiPath Platform. We’re proud to co-innovate with UiPath and excited to shape the future of work alongside them.”

“I’m thrilled to congratulate Capitalize on its recognition as a UiPath Agentic Automation Fast Track Partner,” said Ashim Gupta, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer at UiPath. “Our partners play a critical role in the agentic future of UiPath, from identifying use cases to providing product feedback to co-innovating to help solve customer challenges. IDC forecasts a total market opportunity for agentic automation of $14 billion by 2028. As the market rapidly expands, partners can shape it and seize a first mover advantage. Capitalize has earned this distinction by receiving hands-on training with the UiPath PlatformTMfor agentic automation and establishing their commitment to ushering in the agentic era for customers.”

Capitalize helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Their team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors, like Alteryx, BlackLine, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, ABBYY, Workday Adaptive, and others. Their clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize is SOC 2 Type II certified and works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Their offerings can help every functional area including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. For more information, please visit capitalizeconsulting.com

pages.title: How can WAM help me with Change Control and Versioning on my Alteryx Workflows? - Capitalize Analytics
pages.description: Learn how WAM improves Alteryx workflow change control and versioning by enhancing tracking, governance, and collaboration across teams., Learn how WAM improves Alteryx workflow change control and versioning by enhancing tracking, governance, and collaboration across teams.
pages.og_title: How can WAM help me with Change Control and Versioning on my Alteryx Workflows? - Capitalize Analytics

headings.text: How can WAM help me with Change Control and Versioning on my Alteryx Workflows?
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency

body_copy: Learn how WAM improves Alteryx workflow change control and versioning by enhancing tracking, governance, and collaboration across teams.

Keeping track of the changes made to your workflows can be difficult, especially as they are being developed and tested iteratively. Saving multiple copies of workflows can also be confusing and clutter workspaces, and it can be difficult to distinguish the differences between each version. For example, an analyst has been working on a very complicated workflow, saving different versions after every large change to maintain some sense of version control, as well as the ability to reverse any unnecessary or incorrect changes. After a while, the folder containing the workflow has ten versions of the same workflow, with no way to determine the differences between them without going through each of them individually. Documenting the changes made every update to a workflow time consuming and subject to human error, so it would be helpful if this process could be automated to ensure accuracy and save time.

WAM allows users to track changes made to specific workflows through “commits” similarly to Git or any other version control system. To use this feature, simply navigate to the “Track Changes” section of  WAM and click “Add” to add a workflow. The workflows tracked using this feature of WAM do not have to be in the Workflow Path specified in the Settings like the workflows used in the Reporting or Mass Update sections.

Once a workflow is being tracked, select the workflow under Tracked Workflows and click “Commit” to manually save the current version of the workflow. WAM will also parse the current version of each workflow being tracked every five minutes, so manual commits are not necessary. You will be able to view each saved version of the workflow by selecting the workflow as well. To stop tracking a workflow, click the “Stop Tracking” button. This will remove all saved versions aside from the “Main” version, so it is important to ensure that previous versions will not be needed or are already downloaded before performing this action.

After selecting a tracked workflow, users can select past versions of workflows and download them individually. Versions downloaded this way are downloaded to the “Version Copies” folder that is made in the same folder as the original workflow. Additionally, users can choose to restore a past version as the “main” version – reverting the workflow back to the selected version. To view the changes made between versions, a Change History Report can be run by clicking “Download History.” These reports have three sections: Change History, Summary, and Details.

The Change History section lists each version of the workflow as well as when it was committed. This allows users to quickly get a timeframe as to when each version was saved, which can help narrow down which version that they are looking for.

In the Summary section, users can see a brief overview of the changes made between each saved version and the one before it. Any tool additions, deletions, or updates are listed here, but no specific tool configurations are shown in this section. This allows users to easily view when certain tools are added and is especially useful when branches are added to or removed from workflows.

The Details section of the report shows the configurations of the new or updated tools in a format similar to an Audit Report. This allows users to look for specific changes made to workflows, such as the changing of a formula or data type without having to compare audit reports side by side. Instead, this report shows each line that has changed between the current and previous version’s audit reports, even if they have not been run. With the workflow tracking feature of WAM, documenting and keeping track of changes to workflows is easy since version differences are automatically documented whenever a Change History report is run. The time normally spent on documenting changes to workflows and maintaining specific versions can then be spent on more meaningful tasks, such as improving the workflow and analyzing the results.

headings.text: Demystifying Alteryx: Why It’s Important for Finance Teams and How to Get Started
The Process of Identifying Use Cases
AI in HR: 3 High-Impact Use Cases That Deliver Measurable ROI in Weeks
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency

body_copy: Learn how Alteryx can transform finance teams by automating complex tasks and providing real-time insights. Learn about key use cases, challenges, and a structured approach to implementation in this video.

In the dynamic world of finance, professionals are constantly striving to do more with less. The pressures of faster month-end closes, efficient in-house tax filings, and managing the intricacies of financial data demand robust tools and processes. Tools like Alteryx can be a powerful platform to help transform the Office of Finance by automating and simplifying complex tasks, ensuring compliance, and providing real-time insights.
Travis Haworth, CPA leader of Capitalize’s Finance Transformation practice, discusses these topics in a recent webinar, highlighting the crucial role Alteryx plays in modern finance operations.
Demystifying Alteryx: Why It's Important for Finance Teams and How to Get Started from Capitalize on Vimeo
Finance teams often grapple with several pervasive challenges:
Spreadsheets with Manually Intensive Processes

Challenge: Finance professionals frequently rely on spreadsheets for various tasks, making the process manually intensive and error-prone. The reliance on individual expertise creates risks when key personnel are unavailable.

Use Case: Automating these processes with Alteryx ensures consistency, reduces errors, and saves time. For instance, repetitive tasks like reconciliations and consolidations can be automated, freeing up valuable time for strategic analysis.
Challenge: Detailed process documentation is often lacking or overly complicated, making it difficult for new employees to get up to speed or for existing employees to cover for one another.

Use Case: Alteryx allows for the creation of repeatable workflows with built-in documentation. This means that even if a team member is unavailable or leaves, the processes they developed can be easily understood and replicated by others.
Managing Large Financial Data Sets from Multiple Source Systems
Challenge: As financial transactions grow in volume and complexity, handling large datasets from various sources becomes increasingly challenging.
Use Case: Alteryx excels at blending and analyzing large data sets, enabling finance teams to perform transaction-level analysis without the limitations of traditional spreadsheet software.
Governance, Controls, Security, and Compliance Activities
Challenge: Ensuring proper governance, control, and compliance across financial activities is critical but can be complex and time-consuming.

Use Case: Alteryx provides robust governance and security features, ensuring that data access is controlled and compliant with regulations. It helps maintain an audit trail of activities, facilitating easier compliance management.
Initiatives for Automation and Real-Time Insights
Challenge: Many finance departments aim to automate processes and gain real-time insights, but implementing the necessary technology can be daunting.

Use Case: Alteryx simplifies automation, enabling finance teams to quickly implement and scale automated solutions. Real-time data processing and analysis become more accessible, driving timely and informed decision-making.
When adopting Alteryx, it’s crucial to have a structured approach to identify and develop use cases effectively. The “Crawl, Walk, Run, Fly” methodology is a proven strategy to ensure success.
Approach: Begin with low-hanging fruit – simple, yet impactful tasks. Identify areas where Alteryx can replace manual processes, such as data preparation and consolidation.
Example: Automate the process of pulling data from multiple systems into a unified format, reducing manual effort and errors.
Approach: As familiarity with Alteryx grows, start automating more complex processes. Look for opportunities to streamline workflows that involve multiple steps and systems.
Example: Convert complex Excel workbooks used for monthly reconciliations into streamlined Alteryx workflows that execute automatically.
Approach: Integrate Alteryx with other systems like ERPs, CRMs, and data warehouses to enhance data flow and accessibility. Focus on creating interconnected processes that drive efficiency.
Example: Integrate financial data from ERP systems with CRM data for comprehensive financial reporting and analysis.
Approach: With solid foundations, focus on advanced analytics and innovative use cases. Leverage Alteryx’s capabilities for predictive analytics, geospatial analysis, and other advanced applications.
Example: Implement predictive models to forecast financial performance, identify trends, and make proactive business decisions.
By following this phased approach, finance teams can maximize the benefits of Alteryx, achieving quick wins and building momentum towards more sophisticated and impactful use cases.

Alteryx can be a game-changer for the Office of Finance, addressing common challenges and enabling finance professionals to achieve more with less. However, it’s important to note that software alone doesn’t solve the problem. Successful implementation is critical, and this is where the Office of Finance team at Capitalize can help. Whether it’s helping to identify a plan with a Use Case Assessment or assisting with the actual implementation and training of your team, our expertise ensures you can fully leverage the power of Alteryx for your finance operations.

headings.text: Alteryx Users: Streamline Your External Audit with WAM with Insights from KPMG

body_copy: Organizations utilizing Alteryx to automate critical accounting processes will inevitably find their workflows come under internal and external audit.
Alteryx Users: Streamline Your External Audit with WAM with Insights from KPMG from Capitalize on Vimeo

Organizations utilizing Alteryx to automate critical accounting processes will inevitably find their workflows come under internal and external audit. Most organizations spend WEEKS creating audit support and fielding questions and requests from the auditors, and often still find themselves receiving pushback on their process, controls, and documentation.

Public accounting audit and assurance teams all have varying degrees of familiarity with and understanding of Alteryx. Some may be very comfortable with Alteryx; others may see Alteryx as a “dangerous black box.” No matter their comfort level with Alteryx, any uncertainty or irregularities in your process, controls, or documentation is going to raise major red flags.

Governance frameworks, internal controls, assertion testing and validation reporting, and process documentation are all critical materials that are required to support your Alteryx usage. There hasn’t been an efficient, repeatable, and proven way to capture and create audit support documentation… UNTIL NOW!
WAM takes an audit-centric focus for its one-click audit-ready Alteryx documentation, including:
Detailed Workflow Process Documentation: every input, every output, every single tool
Comprehensive Workflow History Reports: user workflow access, data source access, run history, version change history, development to production approvals
Workflow Development Recommendations: best practices for creating efficient workflows
Dozens of Additional Utilities: eliminating human error when administering hundreds or thousands of Alteryx jobs at scale

Hear from our featured guest Erin Mitchell, KPMG Audit Manager, as she shares her experiences providing Audit and Assurance services for Alteryx customers and how WAM solves many roadblocks during external audits.
Interested in learning more about WAM? Email us at info@capitalizeconsulting.com to get started!

body_copy: Former Stanley Black & Decker CFO Russ Mills shares insights on leveraging Alteryx for finance teams. Discover how Alteryx can streamline data management, enhance analytics, and transform the finance function by enabling strategic decision-making and improving operational efficiency.

In a recent webinar with the Capitalize team, Russ Mills, current Field CFO at Alteryx and former CFO of Stanley Black & Decker, shared his invaluable experiences and insights on leveraging Alteryx for finance teams.

Russ Mills joined Alteryx in 2022, bringing with him a wealth of experience from his 17-year tenure at Stanley Black & Decker, a Fortune 300 company known for its acquisitive nature and complex operational landscape. Stanley Black & Decker, with its $16 billion global footprint, provided fertile ground for Mills to delve into various aspects of financial management, including FP&A, M&A, and analytics.

One of the critical challenges Mills faced at Stanley Black & Decker was managing a sprawling IT landscape with over 115 ERP systems, a number that, although reduced to around 70 today, still posed significant complexity. Despite the temptation to delay analytics projects until new systems were implemented, Mills emphasized the importance of not letting complexity hinder progress. He advocated for jumping into data analysis regardless of where it resides, leveraging platforms like Alteryx to navigate and simplify data integration.

Mills highlighted several key use cases where Alteryx significantly outperforms traditional tools like Excel. These use cases demonstrate Alteryx’s capability to handle large datasets, integrate external data sources, and perform sophisticated analyses.
Handling Large Datasets: Alteryx processes vast amounts of data efficiently, eliminating the limitations of Excel.
Integrating External Data Sources: Combines internal and external data sources seamlessly for richer insights.
Enabling Prescriptive Analytics: Transforms descriptive analyses into prescriptive ones, helping finance teams anticipate trends and make informed decisions.

A significant theme in Mills’ talk was the concept of “flipping the script” in the finance function. Traditionally, finance teams spend the bulk of their time wrangling data, leaving little room for meaningful analysis. Mills advocated for a paradigm shift where finance professionals spend less time on data preparation and more on valuable analyses. This shift not only enhances the quality of insights but also makes the work more engaging and impactful.
Mills outlined four main types of value that Alteryx brings to an organization:
Operational leverage and margin improvement

By freeing up time and resources, Alteryx allows finance teams to focus on high-impact activities, driving margin improvements and reducing operational risks. He emphasized that time saved through automation and efficient data processing translates directly into financial benefits, creating a “pot of gold” at the end of the efficiency rainbow.

To successfully integrate Alteryx into an organization, Mills recommended a strategic approach starting with a small team and gradually scaling up. Here are the key steps for successful implementation and scaling:
Start Small: Begin with a focused team to implement high-value use cases.
Executive Sponsorship: Secure support from top-level executives to champion the initiative.
Change Management: Implement a robust change management process to guide the transition.
Training Programs: Develop training programs to equip employees with the necessary skills.
Showcase Wins: Highlight early successes to generate interest and build momentum.
Scale Gradually: Expand the implementation across the organization based on initial successes.
Mills identified three essential elements for achieving success with Alteryx:
1. Collaboration: Foster cross-functional teamwork.
2. Community: Build internal user groups and leverage the Alteryx community.
3. Culture: Cultivate innovation and continuous improvement.

To succeed with Alteryx, remember to think big, start small, and act fast. By focusing on quick wins and leveraging them to create interest and momentum, you can transform your organization’s approach to data analytics and financial operations.

headings.text: Automation for Accountants: How BlackLine, Alteryx, and UiPath Can Shave Days Off Your Financial Close

body_copy: Accelerate your financial close with technologies like BlackLine, Alteryx, and UiPath. Streamline reconciliations and reduce closing time efficiently.
Automation for Accountants: How to Shave Days Off Your Financial Close Process from Capitalize on Vimeo

Accounting departments always struggle to complete financial close faster. That usually means longer hours or added headcount, but that creates problems like employee burnout, attrition, and inaccuracies.
Many of the tasks slowing down financial close could and should be automated. Put the tedious close processes on the computers and free up accountants to focus on exceptions and analysis.

Capitalize’s team of CPAs and finance professionals help our clients use automation technology like BlackLine, Alteryx, and UiPath to shave days off financial close. We see our clients turn to automation for key financial close processes including:
Accrual and prepaid account reconciliations
Full population transaction matching for bank statements
Accounts receivable and accounts payable aging reporting

In this webinar, we will deep dive into real-life use cases of automation saving time, mitigating risk, avoiding increased headcount, increasing employee satisfaction, and solving financial close challenges, including:
Transaction level bank statement matching, reconciliation, and journal entry
Predicted revenue accrual analysis, journal entry approval, and automated journal entry
Month-end payroll reconciliation from HRIS and General Ledger and reconciliation sign-off process

“Capitalize helped us automate our chaotic month-end close reconciliations. Instead of having to hire more employees to get through our task list, we actually reduced the workload of our existing team!”-Controller at Utility Company

headings.text: Capitalize Analytics Announces Major Enhancements in Scheduling and Orchestrating Workflows for Workflow Admin Manager
Capitalize Recognized as a UiPath 2024 Partner of the Year Award Winner
5 Steps to Unlocking Value from Alteryx Customer Managed Telemetry (CMT)
Workflow Admin Manager (WAM): How to Solve for Alteryx External Audit and IT Efficiency

body_copy: Capitalize Analytics announced today two new features to their Workflow Admin Manager (WAM) platform: WAM Orchestration and Scheduler.

DALLAS, TEXAS, UNITED STATES, December 19, 2022 / EINPresswire.com/ — Capitalize Analytics announced today two new features to their Workflow Admin Manager (WAM) platform: WAM Orchestration and Scheduler. The two additional features increase the ability for companies to plan to execute workflows around server maintenance schedules and chain workflows together based on a series of events.

WAM Scheduler enhances how users create, modify, and view schedules in the Alteryx Gallery. Until now, users were unable to view Alteryx schedules across the entire organization and were limited in their ability to consider resources, new scheduling, maintenance windows, and more in their decision-making process. The schedules are now displayed in a convenient calendar format, allowing users to easily identify scheduling hot spots or gaps and adjust with the drag-and-drop function. WAM Scheduler also enables administrators to move workflows to accommodate maintenance conflicts. In the event that server maintenance disrupts workflows, an administrator can create a maintenance window that will notify users when their workflow hit that window. This makes administering servers seamless in terms of schedules.

“Customers have often expressed how challenging it can be to find a convenient time to take down the analytics infrastructure long enough to do maintenance or upgrades. To top it off, coordination among different teams to reschedule or delay all the automated analytics processes can be painful,” states John Pelletier, Senior Cloud Apps PM at Alteryx. “To have a maintenance window management feature should make a lot of Server admins’ dreams come true.”

WAM Orchestrator allows users to chain workflows together and trigger subsequent workflows based on the success or failure of the one prior. For example, does your pay run populate a series of staging tables that feed to a data warehouse and create journal entries in your ERP system? Does a separate Alteryx workflow owner control each workflow? Now you can chain these workflows together in a matter of minutes in an easy-to-interpret, drag-and-drop interface and see all the historical runs.

“These features are highly beneficial to the administrator, who needs to consume organizational Alteryx scheduling and make quick, informed decisions, as well as the business user who needs to have a myriad of workflows trigger based on the actions of another workflow,” says Christina McLuckie, Workflow Admin Manager Product Director. “WAM Server is your one-stop shop for your Alteryx governance and administrative needs.”

Workflow Admin Manager is a tool that gives Alteryx users and team the ability to manage, document, orchestrate, schedule, and audit hundreds of workflows effectively. For more info, visit capitalizeconsulting.com/WAM.

Capitalize Analytics helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Our team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors like Alteryx, BlackLine, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, ABBYY, Workday Adaptive, and others. Our clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize Analytics works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Our offerings can help every functional area, including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. For more information, please visit capitalizeanalytics.com

headings.text: Casabase Software and Capitalize Analytics Partner to Bring Clients Faster Access to Analytics with Oracle ERP and EPM
Capitalize Recognized as a UiPath 2024 Partner of the Year Award Winner
Capitalize Named Alteryx Americas and Marketplace Partner of the Year
Unleash the Power of Oracle BI Publisher and Power BI Integration with ExoInsight

body_copy: Capitalize Analytics announce partnership that enables clients access financial and business data stored in Oracle software.

DALLAS , TEXAS, UNITER STATES, October 28, 2022 / EINPresswire.com/ — Capitalize Analytics and Casabase Software announced this week that a new partnership will enable clients to easily access financial and business data stored in Oracle Essbase, Oracle HFM, Oracle Planning Budgeting Cloud Solution (PBCS), Oracle Financial Consolidation and Close Service (FCCS), and Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP.

“Our partnership with Capitalize Analytics is a game-changer for Casabase Software clients. By leveraging their deep expertise and experience in many different analytic and reporting technologies, our clients will now have access to a trusted partner that can help them not only with their existing processes, but also help support future initiatives, identify areas of improvement, and provide guidance on everything from specific ExoInsight questions to overall data strategy,” says Carr Harriman, Chief Operations Officer of Casabase Software. “We are thrilled to offer a more holistic experience and are excited about what this partnership means for current and future Casabase Software clients.”

Casabase Software is the developer of ExoInsight, a platform that provides data access and integration with a variety of Oracle applications. With the ExoInsight Alteryx Tool, users on Alteryx Designer and Alteryx Server can connect to Essbase, HFM, PBCS, FCCS, and Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP, enabling teams to tackle large-scale ERP and EPM analytics and reporting projects with ease.

The office of the CFO depends on both financial and operational data to report on the health of the company, create budgets for future periods, and track progress against those budgets. Even users who have no prior knowledge of these Oracle sources can use ExoInsight to extract the financial and operational data and hierarchies they need from these systems. Combining the ability to more easily access data with the analytic process automation provided by the Alteryx APA Platform™ gives users the ability to provide unparalleled analysis and insights.

“The Capitalize and Casabase Software partnership is incredibly exciting, because we see how companies struggle to access data in Oracle ERP and EPM. Casabase Software has solved that issue with ExoInsight, and it works with Alteryx, Tableau, Power BI, or replicates to relational databases,” states Eric Soden, Managing Partner at Capitalize Analytics. “A formal partnership with Casabase and ExoInsight increases our ability to deliver enterprise solutions to our joint Oracle clients. If Oracle is a key data source in your organization, the ExoInsight solution is a no-brainer.”

Capitalize Analytics helps organizations implement software, automate processes, and analyze data. Their team brings decades of experience to every project as they work with best-in-class technology vendors, like Alteryx, UiPath, Tableau, Snowflake, ABBYY, Workday Adaptive, and others. Their clients can be found coast to coast in the US and Canada. Capitalize Analytics works with small startups and some of the largest companies in the world to increase their efficiency using data automation technology. Their offerings can help every functional area including Accounting, Tax, FP&A, Audit, HR, Marketing, Operations, and any group that is struggling with data or manual processes. Contact info@capitalizeconsulting.com for more information.
